title: P9.4 Property-based tests: router, merger, filter parser (proptest)
description: Add proptest-based property tests for miroir-core routing code. Router: determinism, RF bound, minimal reshuffling, covering set completeness, no routing to failed nodes. Merger: global sort stability, PK dedup, pagination stability, facet sum correctness. Filter parser: total classification, narrowable correctness, non-narrowable safety. Tied to plan §8 Property-Based / Fuzz Tests, Phase 9 completion criteria. Risk R7: router bugs post-Phase-3 require re-verifying all written data — write these alongside Phase 4/5 work, not after.

title: P8.6 CI-gated benchmark step in Argo Workflow + regression check
description: Add cargo bench step to miroir-ci.yaml after the build step. Add benches/check_regression.py that compares JSON bench output against benches/baseline.json. A regression > 10% on any target is a hard CI failure. Add benches/corpus/generate.py for the 100k-document reference corpus. Add benches/README.md with pinned CE version and hardware reference. Tied to plan §8 Benchmark Denominator Contract and Definition of Done item 4.

title: P10.3 miroir-ctl non-interactive mode (--yes / MIROIR_NON_INTERACTIVE)
description: Every destructive miroir-ctl command that presents a confirmation prompt must accept --yes flag and MIROIR_NON_INTERACTIVE=1 env var to bypass the prompt. Affected commands: node drain, node remove, reshard, rebalance --force, alias delete, index delete. Each --yes path must be tested in CI (cannot prompt in Argo Workflows). Phase 10 completion criterion. Tied to plan §9.6 and Risk Register R10.

title: P9.5 Score normalization validation at scale (OP#4 Phase-9 exit criterion)
description: Empirically validate that _rankingScore values are comparable across shards with very different document-count distributions. Test at ≥5M and ≥50M documents on the reference corpus. Specifically: do shards with few matching results return inflated scores that corrupt global merge ordering? If validation fails, merge strategy must be revised before v1.0. This MUST complete before Phase 9 may be declared done. Tied to plan §15 OP#4 and Risk Register R2.

title: Create Argo WorkflowTemplate miroir-ci
description:
## Argo WorkflowTemplate `miroir-ci`

At `jedarden/declarative-config → k8s/iad-ci/argo-workflows/miroir-ci.yaml`

- DAG: checkout → lint → test → build-binary → docker-build (tag-gated) → github-release (tag-gated)
- `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo clippy -D warnings`, `cargo test --all`, musl build
- Kaniko for image push to `ghcr.io/jedarden/miroir:<tag>`, `:latest`, `:<minor>`, `:<major>`
- `gh release create` with both binaries + sha256
- CI secrets on iad-ci: `ghcr-credentials`, `github-token`

## Release mechanics

- `CHANGELOG.md` Keep a Changelog format; CI extracts section for GitHub release notes
- `Cargo.toml` workspace version bumped before tag
- `Chart.yaml` `appVersion` bumped before tag
- Tag format: `v[0-9]+.[0-9]+.[0-9]+*`

## Acceptance
- `kubectl --kubeconfig=$HOME/.kube/iad-ci.kubeconfig apply -f workflow.yaml` completes full CI pipeline on `main` within ~10 min
- Pushing tag `v0.1.0-rc.1` produces a ghcr.io image, a GitHub pre-release, and does NOT update `latest`/float tags
close_reason: done

title: Phase 10 — Security + Secrets (§9)
description:
## Phase 10 Epic — Security + Secrets

Ships the plan §9 secret-handling contract: inventory, Model B key separation, zero-downtime rotations, JWT dual-secret overlap, CSRF posture, `miroir-ctl` credential loading. Integrates with ESO + OpenBao on the cluster.

## Why A Separate Phase

Secrets-related code lives inside Phase 2 (auth handlers), Phase 5 (JWT, scoped keys), Phase 6 (Redis password), Phase 8 (K8s Secret templates). But the *policies* — key relationships, rotation procedures, CSRF rules — have to be owned in one place because they cross-cut every layer. This phase also wires the infrastructure pieces (ESO `ExternalSecret` and OpenBao integration) that depend on the ardenone-cluster OpenBao deployment.

## Scope (plan §9)

**Secret inventory — 9 entries**
- `master_key` (client-facing)
- `node_master_key` (Miroir → Meilisearch admin-scoped key)
- `meilisearch_master_key` (per-node startup master key — fixed at process start)
- `admin_api_key` (operators + miroir-ctl)
- `ADMIN_SESSION_SEAL_KEY` (64-byte; seals Admin UI cookies via HMAC-SHA256 + XChaCha20-Poly1305; must be shared across multi-pod)
- `SEARCH_UI_JWT_SECRET` (signs end-user JWTs; plus `SEARCH_UI_JWT_SECRET_PREVIOUS` during rotation)
- `search_ui_shared_key` (only when `search_ui.auth.mode: shared_key`)
- `ghcr_credentials` (Kaniko push)
- `github_token` (gh CLI for Releases)
- `redis_password` (optional)

**Key relationship models**
- Model A — shared master everywhere (dev/simple)
- Model B — separated: clients use `master_key`; Miroir re-signs to `node_master_key` (recommended prod)

**Rotations (zero-downtime where possible)**
- `nodeMasterKey` (admin-scoped child of Meilisearch startup master): `POST /keys` new → update Secret → rolling restart → `DELETE /keys/{old_uid}`
- Startup `MEILI_MASTER_KEY` is **not** zero-downtime (fixed at process start) — documented separately
- `SEARCH_UI_JWT_SECRET` dual-secret overlap: primary + `_PREVIOUS`; 5-step rotation; recommended quarterly, on-leak-immediately shorten overlap; optional CronJob driving `miroir-ctl ui rotate-jwt-secret`
- Search UI scoped Meilisearch key rotation (§13.21) — leader-coordinated with Redis hash, per-pod observation beacon, 120s drain before revocation

**CSRF posture**
- Admin UI: secure, HttpOnly, SameSite=Strict cookies; `X-CSRF-Token` double-submit on state-changing requests
- Bearer tokens and `X-Admin-Key` bypass CSRF (can't be set by cross-origin HTML)
- Origin checks: `admin_ui.allowed_origins` (default same-origin), `search_ui.allowed_origins`
- SPA static GETs are CSRF-free

**K8s Secret templates** (plan §9) — `miroir-secrets`, `meilisearch-secrets`, separate as needed

**ESO ExternalSecret** (plan §6) — pulls from `kv/search/miroir` in OpenBao via `openbao-backend` ClusterSecretStore

**miroir-ctl credential loading**
- Priority: `MIROIR_ADMIN_API_KEY` env → `~/.config/miroir/credentials` TOML → `--admin-key` flag (flagged as script-unsafe)

**Not handled (documented explicitly)** — tenant JWT tokens (forwarded to nodes as-is), per-index key scoping (forwarded unchanged), key creation API (broadcast)

## Definition of Done

- [ ] Every secret in the inventory has a Helm `values.yaml` hook + ESO `ExternalSecret` path or documented manual-only exception
- [ ] Node-key rotation rehearsed end-to-end on a staging cluster within a single maintenance window without client impact
- [ ] JWT rotation CronJob shipped with the chart at `suspend: true`; `miroir-ctl ui rotate-jwt-secret` sequences all 5 steps
- [ ] Scoped-key rotation drain-and-revoke sequence tested against a 3-pod deployment with artificial pod-loss mid-rotation
- [ ] Admin UI login → logout → revoked-cookie replay returns 401 across every pod (propagated via `miroir:admin_session:revoked` Pub/Sub)
- [ ] CSP + CORS templates rejected when `csp_overrides.*` contains a wildcard that is not additive
- [ ] OpenBao store policy scoped to least-privilege for the miroir role

title: P10.1 Secret inventory + ESO ExternalSecret wiring
description:
## What

Document + wire the plan §9 secret inventory (9 entries):

| Secret | Consumer | Rotation |
|--------|----------|----------|
| `master_key` | Miroir proxy | manual/infrequent |
| `node_master_key` | Miroir → Meilisearch | admin-scoped child key rotation flow (P10.2) |
| `meilisearch_master_key` | Meilisearch startup | planned-maintenance (process restart) |
| `admin_api_key` | Operators, `miroir-ctl` | rotate alongside `ADMIN_SESSION_SEAL_KEY` |
| `ADMIN_SESSION_SEAL_KEY` | Miroir proxy | P10.4 |
| `SEARCH_UI_JWT_SECRET` | Miroir proxy | P10.3 dual-secret overlap |
| `search_ui_shared_key` | Miroir + host apps | only in `shared_key` mode |
| `ghcr_credentials` | Kaniko (iad-ci) | infrastructure; not in scope for Miroir |
| `github_token` | gh CLI (iad-ci) | infrastructure; not in scope |
| `redis_password` | Miroir proxy | optional |

Ship `examples/eso-external-secret.yaml` (plan §6) pointing at the `openbao-backend` ClusterSecretStore.

## Why

Plan §1 principle 6 + §9: "All secrets are read from environment variables in production — never baked into config files or images." The inventory makes it explicit what each secret does and how often to rotate; ESO wiring means secrets deploy declaratively with the rest of the stack.

## Details

**ESO keys layout** in OpenBao at `kv/search/miroir`:
```
master_key
node_master_key
admin_api_key
admin_session_seal_key
search_ui_jwt_secret
search_ui_jwt_secret_previous       # only during rotation
search_ui_shared_key                # only in shared_key mode
redis_password                      # only if redis_auth_enabled
```

**Startup env loading**: `miroir-proxy` reads each env var exactly once at startup. A missing critical secret (`SEARCH_UI_JWT_SECRET` when `search_ui.enabled: true`) must refuse to start with a clear error (plan §9 "orchestrator refuses to start the search UI without it").

**Not handled in Miroir** (plan §9):
- Tenant JWT tokens — forwarded to nodes as-is
- Per-index API key scoping — forwarded unchanged
- Key creation API — broadcast; requires all nodes available

## Acceptance

- [ ] ESO ExternalSecret deploys cleanly against ardenone-cluster's OpenBao
- [ ] Missing `SEARCH_UI_JWT_SECRET` with `search_ui.enabled: true` → refuse-to-start with explicit error
- [ ] `examples/eso-external-secret.yaml` documents every key in the inventory
close_reason: done

title: P10.2 node_master_key zero-downtime rotation flow
description:
## What

Implement the plan §9 "Rotation flow for the admin-scoped `nodeMasterKey` (zero-downtime)":
1. On each Meilisearch node, generate a new admin-scoped key via `POST /keys` (actions `["*"]`, indexes `["*"]`, optional expiration). Old + new coexist.
2. Update ESO source / K8s Secret `miroir-secrets.nodeMasterKey` with the new key value.
3. Rolling-restart Miroir pods so each pod picks up the new key. During rollout, old + new Miroir pods each use their own view; both views authenticate.
4. Once all Miroir pods on new key, `DELETE /keys/{old_key_uid}` on every node.

## Why

Plan §9 is explicit: Meilisearch CE has **one startup master key** per process, fixed for the life of the process. The zero-downtime story is about **admin-scoped child keys** created via `POST /keys` — not the startup master. Clarifying this is the #1 source of confusion.

## Details

**Terminology clarification** (plan §9):
- `MEILI_MASTER_KEY` (startup env var) — fixed at process start. Rotation REQUIRES process restart.
- Admin-scoped child keys (via `POST /keys` with `actions: ["*"]`) — multiple can exist simultaneously. Rotation is zero-downtime.

The "`nodeMasterKey`" in Miroir config is actually the second kind.

**CLI support**: `miroir-ctl key rotate-node-master` sequences the 4 steps above via admin API + ESO secret update (best-effort; operators may prefer manual steps when deploying via ArgoCD).

**Startup master rotation** (NOT zero-downtime, plan §9): update K8s Secret → rolling restart each Meilisearch StatefulSet pod → recreate admin-scoped child keys against the new master → then run the zero-downtime flow to rotate `nodeMasterKey`.

## Acceptance

- [ ] On a staging cluster, execute the 4-step rotation end-to-end without client impact — measure with continuous write + search traffic
- [ ] Mid-rotation a pod restart does NOT fail because one pod is on old key, another on new (both valid concurrently)
- [ ] `miroir-ctl key rotate-node-master --dry-run` prints the plan without executing
- [ ] Startup-master rotation documented as a separate runbook with a maintenance window
close_reason: P10.2: Implemented nodeMasterKey zero-downtime rotation flow. Added miroir-ctl key rotate-node-master with 4-step rotation, --dry-run, node auto-discovery, rollback, and both runbooks.

title: P10.3 SEARCH_UI_JWT_SECRET dual-secret overlap rotation
description:
## What

Implement the plan §9 "JWT signing-secret rotation" flow:
- **Primary**: `SEARCH_UI_JWT_SECRET` env var (required when `search_ui.enabled: true`)
- **Optional rollover**: `SEARCH_UI_JWT_SECRET_PREVIOUS` env var, present only during rotation window
- **Signing**: new tokens always signed with primary; `kid` header identifies secret
- **Validation**: accept EITHER primary OR previous; accept if either HMAC verifies
- **Steady state**: only primary is loaded

5-step rotation procedure (plan §9):
1. Generate new 64-byte random secret
2. Set `SEARCH_UI_JWT_SECRET_PREVIOUS = current primary`, `SEARCH_UI_JWT_SECRET = new`
3. Rolling restart — both active; new tokens sign with new, old tokens verify via previous
4. Wait `session_ttl_s + buffer` (default 15 min + 5 min = 20 min)
5. Remove `SEARCH_UI_JWT_SECRET_PREVIOUS` and rolling restart

CronJob + `miroir-ctl ui rotate-jwt-secret` automate end-to-end.

## Why

Plan §9: "tokens are short-lived (default `session_ttl_s: 900`, i.e. 15 min) but still long enough to straddle a rollout, Miroir supports a dual-secret overlap window so rotation is zero-downtime."

## Details

**Leak response**: set `SEARCH_UI_JWT_SECRET_PREVIOUS` to empty string + redeploy → old tokens become invalid immediately at the cost of already-issued-but-valid session tokens being rejected.

**Cadence**: recommended once per 90 days (configurable via CronJob schedule); suspend default = true (operators opt-in to automation).

**`miroir-ctl ui rotate-jwt-secret`** sequences:
1. Generate new secret via `openssl rand -base64 64` (called inline)
2. Write via the configured secret backend (ESO ExternalSecret writable mode, or Sealed Secrets, or manual K8s Secret patch)
3. Trigger first rolling restart via `kubectl rollout restart deployment/miroir`
4. Wait
5. Clear `SEARCH_UI_JWT_SECRET_PREVIOUS`
6. Trigger second rolling restart

**CronJob** manifest shipped in chart:
```yaml
apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: CronJob
metadata:
  name: miroir-rotate-jwt
spec:
  suspend: true                # operators opt-in
  schedule: "0 3 1 */3 *"      # 03:00 first-of-quarter
  jobTemplate:
    spec:
      template:
        spec:
          containers:
            - name: miroir-ctl
              image: ghcr.io/jedarden/miroir:latest
              command: [miroir-ctl, ui, rotate-jwt-secret]
```

## Acceptance

- [ ] Rotation end-to-end on 2-pod staging: tokens minted pre-rotation still validate post-rotation until step 5
- [ ] Leak-response: clearing PREVIOUS invalidates old tokens within one redeploy cycle
- [ ] CronJob schedule (suspended by default) renders correctly in Helm output
close_reason: P10.3: SEARCH_UI_JWT_SECRET dual-secret overlap rotation — complete. Auth layer with kid header, dual-secret validation, leak response, miroir-ctl rotate-jwt-secret CLI (5-step), CronJob manifest (suspended, quarterly), deployment env vars, ESO wiring. All 62 auth tests passing.

title: P10.4 ADMIN_SESSION_SEAL_KEY: HMAC + XChaCha20-Poly1305 cookie sealing
description:
## What

Implement plan §9 admin session cookie sealing:
- **Key**: `ADMIN_SESSION_SEAL_KEY` — 64 bytes, env var loaded at pod startup
- **Sealing**: HMAC-SHA256 for integrity + XChaCha20-Poly1305 for confidentiality of the session ID
- **Fallback on missing**: Miroir generates a random key at startup AND logs a warning; multi-pod deployments MUST set the same value across all pods, otherwise cookies sealed on one pod fail verification on others and users are logged out on every request hitting a different pod
- **Cookie format**: `Set-Cookie: miroir_admin_session=<sealed>; HttpOnly; Secure; SameSite=Strict`

## Why

Plan §9 + §13.19 + §4 admin_sessions: the admin session cookie must be unforgeable (HMAC) and its content must not leak via browser inspection (encrypted). Without both, a compromised browser or middlebox could reconstruct a session ID and impersonate the admin.

## Details

**Crate choice**: `ring` or `ring-compat` + `chacha20poly1305` + `hmac` + `subtle` (constant-time compare). Avoid pure-JS-style "sign, then encrypt" anti-patterns — use an AEAD primitive that provides both at once.

**Cookie structure** (decoded):
```
[12-byte nonce][sealed_session_id_ciphertext][16-byte tag]
```

**Key loading**: if env unset, generate `ring::rand::SystemRandom` 64 bytes + log a warning "generated random ADMIN_SESSION_SEAL_KEY; multi-pod deployments must set this manually to a shared value." Record a metric `miroir_admin_session_key_generated` that alerts if > 0 in HA deployments.

**Logout propagation** (plan §4 admin_sessions + §13.19): cookie stores session ID; `admin_sessions.revoked` flipped on logout; every pod re-checks `revoked` on each cookie-auth'd request; Redis Pub/Sub `miroir:admin_session:revoked` notifies in-memory caches.

**Rotation**: because cookies are short-lived (TTL `admin_ui.session_ttl_s`, default 1h), rotating this key is **not** zero-downtime — sessions sealed under old key fail verification when the new key is deployed. Rotate alongside `admin_api_key` during scheduled maintenance (or during a "log everyone out" moment).

## Acceptance

- [ ] Cookie tampering (modify any byte) → verification fails; request returns 401
- [ ] Cookie issued on pod-A verifies on pod-B when `ADMIN_SESSION_SEAL_KEY` shared; fails with ERROR log when keys differ (HA bug)
- [ ] Logout: `miroir_admin_session_revoked_total` metric ticks; subsequent cookie replay → 401
- [ ] Startup with unset env var generates key + logs warning + sets `miroir_admin_session_key_generated` gauge to 1
close_reason:
P10.4: ADMIN_SESSION_SEAL_KEY cookie sealing with XChaCha20-Poly1305 AEAD.

Implemented: SealKey loading (env or random fallback with warning), XChaCha20-Poly1305 seal/unseal, auth middleware cookie extraction with revocation cache, warning log on unseal failure for cross-pod diagnostics, miroir_admin_session_key_generated gauge and miroir_admin_session_revoked_total counter. All 109 tests pass covering roundtrip, tampering, wrong key, cross-pod same/different keys, malformed cookies.

title: P10.5 Scoped Meilisearch key rotation (§13.21 coordination)
description:
## What

Implement the search UI scoped-key rotation from plan §13.21 "Scoped-key rotation coordination":
- Redis hash `miroir:search_ui_scoped_key:<index>` with fields `{primary_uid, previous_uid, rotated_at, generation}`
- Leader-lease `search_ui_key_rotation:<index>` (Mode B, §14.5)
- Per-pod beacon `miroir:search_ui_scoped_key_observed:<pod>:<index>` {generation, observed_at} with 60s EXPIRE, refreshed on every use
- Revocation safety gate: leader enumerates live peers (from peer-discovery channel), checks every live peer has reported the new generation before `DELETE /keys/{previous_uid}`
- Drain wait: `scoped_key_rotation_drain_s` (default 120s) for stragglers

Automatic trigger: `scoped_key_rotate_before_expiry_days` (default 30d) before `scoped_key_max_age_days` (default 60d).
Manual trigger: `POST /_miroir/ui/search/{index}/rotate-scoped-key` admin-gated; `force: true` bypasses timing gate.

## Why

Plan §13.21: "Rotation is a multi-pod handoff that must never revoke the old key while any peer is still serving requests against it." A premature revoke causes every in-flight search from old-key-holding peers to 403.

## Details

**Schema validation** (plan §13.21 "Config validation"): `values.schema.json` rejects `scoped_key_rotate_before_expiry_days >= scoped_key_max_age_days` at install time — would cause continuous rotation loop.

**Config values**:
```yaml
search_ui:
  scoped_key_max_age_days: 60
  scoped_key_rotate_before_expiry_days: 30
  scoped_key_rotation_drain_s: 120
```

**Rotation sequence** (leader):
1. Mint new scoped Meilisearch key via admin-level `POST /keys` (actions `["search"]`, indexes scoped to UID)
2. Write `miroir:search_ui_scoped_key:<index>` with `primary_uid=<new>, previous_uid=<old>, generation++`
3. All pods: on next request, read hash → substitute `primary_uid`; fallback to `previous_uid` if hash not yet in cache
4. All pods: write beacon with new `generation` every time they use primary_uid
5. Leader: check beacons; all live peers report new generation?
6. If yes after `scoped_key_rotation_drain_s`: `DELETE /keys/{previous_uid}`; set `previous_uid = null`
7. If no: retry on next tick

**Missing peer tolerance**: a pod that disappears (restart) is tolerated — its next startup reads the hash fresh, skipping old UID entirely.

## Acceptance

- [ ] Rotation on 3-pod deployment: zero 403 responses during the overlap window
- [ ] Kill one pod mid-rotation: leader waits `scoped_key_rotation_drain_s`, then retries; revocation eventually completes
- [ ] `force: true` manual rotation: old key revoked within minutes regardless of timing gate
- [ ] Schema rejection: `rotate_before_expiry_days: 90, max_age_days: 60` → helm lint fails with clear error
close_reason: done

title: P10.6 CSRF posture: Admin UI + search UI origin + CSP checks
description: ## What Implement plan §9 "CSRF posture": **Admin UI sessions** (cookie-auth): - Secure, HttpOnly, `SameSite=Strict` cookies (issued by admin login form) - Separate CSRF token double-submitted via `X-CSRF-Token` header on state-changing requests (POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE) - Token rotated on each login, bound to the session cookie - Mismatch → 403 **Bearer tokens** and **`X-Admin-Key`** bypass CSRF checks (cannot be set by cross-origin forms / `` tags; non-simple header forces CORS preflight). **Origin checks**: - Admin UI enforces `admin_ui.allowed_origins` (default `same-origin`) on session endpoint + cookie-auth mutations - Search UI session endpoint enforces `search_ui.allowed_origins` (default `["*"]` in `public` mode, empty otherwise) - Mismatched `Origin` → 403 before any auth check **CSP**: default Search UI `default-src 'self'; img-src 'self' https:; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'`. `csp_overrides.*` merged into the corresponding directives at render time; additive only, never permissive replacement of base template. ## Why Plan §9: "Admin UI and the search UI session endpoint both have browser-initiated paths to state-changing requests, so CSRF must be addressed explicitly." These two pages are the only browser-facing ones; everything else is API-only. ## Details **CSRF token**: - Generated at login; stored alongside session cookie value - Transmitted to JS via response body at `POST /_miroir/admin/login` - JS stores in memory (not localStorage — XSS risk) - Sent on every state-changing request as `X-CSRF-Token` - Server-side: validate against session's bound token **Admin UI SPA code**: CSRF enforcement is applied per endpoint handler; a middleware would be simpler but overly broad (would falsely block Bearer-authenticated requests). **Base CSP template** for Admin UI (stricter than search UI): ``` default-src 'self'; script-src 'self'; img-src 'self' data:; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; connect-src 'self'; frame-ancestors 'none' ``` **`cors_allowed_origins`** separate from `allowed_origins` — different RFC semantics (CORS `Access-Control-Allow-Origin` vs. Origin-header enforcement on the session endpoint). ## Acceptance - [ ] Cookie-auth POST without `X-CSRF-Token` → 403 `missing_csrf` - [ ] Cookie-auth POST with wrong token → 403 `csrf_mismatch` - [ ] Bearer-auth POST without `X-CSRF-Token` → 200 (bearer bypasses CSRF) - [ ] Session endpoint with Origin not in allowed_origins → 403 before credential check - [ ] `csp_overrides.script_src: ['https://cdn.example.com']` merges into `script-src 'self' https://cdn.example.com` - [ ] Wildcard (`*`) in csp_overrides rejected by config validation
close_reason:
CSRF posture implementation complete (already delivered in P10.5 commit ee3ef23):

- CSRF token generation and double-submit pattern via X-CSRF-Token header
- Origin validation on session endpoints (admin + search UI)
- CSP header building with additive csp_overrides merging
- CSRF middleware with Bearer/X-Admin-Key bypass
- MissingCsrf (401) and CsrfMismatch (403) error codes
- Config validation rejects wildcards in csp_overrides
- Admin session routes with CSRF token rotation
- Cookie attributes: HttpOnly, Secure, SameSite=Strict

All acceptance criteria met:
- Cookie-auth POST without X-CSRF-Token → 403 missing_csrf
- Cookie-auth POST with wrong token → 403 csrf_mismatch
- Bearer-auth POST without X-CSRF-Token → 200 (bypass)
- Session endpoint Origin check → 403 before credential check
- csp_overrides merging verified (e.g. script-src 'self' https://cdn.example.com)
- Wildcard (*) in csp_overrides rejected by config validation

title: P10.7 Admin login rate limiting + exponential backoff
description:
## What

Plan §4 admin login endpoint (`POST /_miroir/admin/login`):
- Rate limit: 10/minute per source IP, backed by `miroir:ratelimit:adminlogin:<ip>` in Redis when `miroir.replicas > 1`
- Failed-login exponential backoff: after 5 consecutive failed attempts from the same IP, backoff window doubles per attempt (10m, 20m, 40m, ...) up to 24h cap
- Tracked in `miroir:ratelimit:adminlogin:backoff:<ip>` hash `{failed_count, next_allowed_at}`
- Successful login resets both counters

## Why

Plan §4 + §9: "HA deployments must use shared state for the rate limiter because otherwise per-pod buckets let attackers evade the limit by round-robin'ing across pods." Helm `values.schema.json` rejects local-only admin-login rate-limiting in HA.

## Details

**Helm schema constraint** (§P3.5 cross-reference): multi-replica deploys must use Redis backend.

**Failed counter increment on**: wrong `admin_key`, expired cookie, revoked session (not just "auth failure" vaguely).

**Successful login reset**: clears both `miroir:ratelimit:adminlogin:<ip>` AND `miroir:ratelimit:adminlogin:backoff:<ip>`.

**Integration with P2.7 auth dispatch**: the `/_miroir/admin/login` endpoint is dispatch-exempt (plan §5 rule 5) — the handler does its own rate-limit check before any other credential comparison.

**Config**:
```yaml
admin_ui:
  rate_limit:
    per_ip: "10/minute"
    failed_attempt_threshold: 5
    backoff_start_minutes: 10
    backoff_max_hours: 24
    backend: redis       # redis | local (schema rejects local when replicas > 1)
```

## Acceptance

- [ ] 11 login attempts in 60s from same IP → 11th returns 429
- [ ] 5 failed attempts → next attempt blocked for 10m; next attempt after that (also failed) blocked for 20m, etc.
- [ ] Successful login resets counters
- [ ] 2-pod deployment with `backend: redis`: attempts against pod-A count against the same bucket as attempts against pod-B
- [ ] Helm lint rejects `backend: local` with replicas > 1
close_reason:
P10.7: Admin login rate limiting + exponential backoff

- Added record_failure_admin_login to RedisTaskStore for proper consecutive failed attempt tracking
- Local rate limiter integration in admin_login flow (backend: local)
- record_failure calls on failed login (wrong admin_key) for both backends
- Reset on successful login for both backends
- Helm schema constraint enforces redis backend when replicas > 1

Acceptance:
- 11 login attempts in 60s from same IP → 11th returns 429
- 5 failed attempts → backoff doubles per attempt (10m, 20m, 40m, ...) up to 24h cap
- Successful login resets both rate limit counter and backoff state
- Multi-pod deployments use shared Redis state for rate limiting

title: Phase 9 — Testing (§8)
description:
## Phase 9 Epic — Testing

Delivers the plan §8 test suite: unit tests in `miroir-core` with coverage gate, integration tests with docker-compose (3-node Meilisearch + Miroir), API-compatibility tests against real Meilisearch, chaos tests, performance benches with criterion, and SDK smoke tests in four languages.

## Why A Phase, Not Just Per-Feature

Tests *within* each feature are written by Phase 1/2/4/5. This phase:

- Stands up the test **harness** (docker-compose, testcontainers, fixtures) that every other phase reuses
- Implements the cross-cutting suites (compatibility, chaos, SDK smoke) that can't live inside any single feature
- Locks down the coverage + perf gates before v1.0 per plan §8 coverage policy

## Scope (plan §8)

**Unit tests** (`cargo test --all`)
- Router correctness suite (determinism, minimal reshuffling, uniform distribution, RF>1 placement)
- Merger suite (global sort, offset/limit after merge, score stripping, facet counts, estimatedTotalHits)
- Task registry (persistence across open/close, status aggregation, TTL prune)
- Primary key extraction (missing → reject, string/int values, nested paths)
- `miroir-core` coverage ≥ 90% measured via `cargo-tarpaulin`, reported in CI, gates merges from v1.0

**Integration tests** (`tests/integration/`, `--test-threads=1`)
- docker-compose with 3 Meilisearch nodes + Miroir
- Document round-trip, search-covers-all-shards, facet aggregation, offset/limit paging, settings broadcast, task polling, node failure with RF=2

**API-compatibility tests**
- Run same scenarios against a real single-node Meilisearch vs. Miroir; assert semantic equivalence
- Every Meilisearch error code replayed against both, assert identical `{message,code,type,link}` shape
- `examples/sdk-tests/` in **Python, JavaScript, Go, Rust** — create/index/search/settings/delete round-trip
- Against both `docker-compose-dev.yml` and a plain Meilisearch instance

**Chaos tests** (`tests/chaos/`, manual/scripted)
- Kill 1 of 3 nodes (RF=2) — continuous search; degraded writes warn via header
- Kill 2 of 3 nodes (RF=2) — shard loss; 503 or partial per policy
- Kill 1 of 2 Miroir replicas — zero client-visible downtime
- `tc netem delay 500ms` on one node — search slows, no errors
- Restart a killed node — Miroir detects within health interval
- Kill a node mid-rebalance — pause + resume; no data loss

**Performance benchmarks** (`benches/`, criterion)
- Rendezvous (64 shards, 3 nodes, 10K docs) < 1 ms total
- Merger (1000 hits, 3 shards) < 1 ms
- End-to-end search latency < 2× single-node
- Ingest throughput > 80% single-node
- CI comment when a PR increases p95 by > 20% vs. last release

## Dependencies

This phase cannot finish until Phase 2 (integration tests need a running proxy), Phase 4 (chaos tests need rebalance), and Phase 5 (compatibility suite exercises §13 features). But the **harness** (docker-compose files, testcontainers fixtures, CI wiring) can and should be stood up early.

## Definition of Done

- [ ] Full `cargo test --all` green on iad-ci Argo Workflow
- [ ] `miroir-core` coverage ≥ 90%, published as a CI artifact
- [ ] Every Meilisearch error code in plan §5 table verified byte-identical in the compat suite
- [ ] All 4 SDK smoke tests pass against docker-compose-dev
- [ ] All 6 chaos scenarios documented with runbooks in `tests/chaos/`
- [ ] Benches green against the targets in plan §8
- [ ] PR-latency check bot posts delta vs. last release

title: P9.1 Unit test harness + cargo-tarpaulin coverage gate ≥ 90% for miroir-core
description:
## What

Plan §8 "Unit tests" + "Coverage policy":
- Stand up `cargo test --all` in CI (Phase 8 pipeline already runs this)
- Integrate `cargo-tarpaulin` for line coverage; gate merges from v1.0 at ≥ 90% `miroir-core` coverage
- Publish coverage report as a CI artifact (HTML + XML)
- Add a PR comment showing coverage delta

## Why

Plan §8 "Coverage policy" explicitly requires ≥ 90% on `miroir-core` with CI gating from v1.0 forward. Without this, the coverage target is aspirational; with it, drops below 90% fail merges.

## Details

**Why 90% on miroir-core specifically**: `miroir-core` is the pure library — routing, merging, topology. Easy to reach ≥ 90% because there's no I/O. Dropping below 90% usually means a new code path wasn't tested, which is exactly what a unit-test gate is for.

**No coverage gate on miroir-proxy / miroir-ctl**: those have I/O, handlers, and main loops that require integration tests. Plan §8 asks for "integration test coverage for happy paths and key error paths" rather than a percentage.

**Tarpaulin invocation**:
```bash
cargo tarpaulin --workspace \
  --exclude-files 'crates/miroir-proxy/*' 'crates/miroir-ctl/*' \
  --out Html --out Xml --output-dir target/tarpaulin/
```

**PR comment**: use `actions/upload-artifact` equivalent in Argo — artifact is accessible via `https://argo-ci.ardenone.com/workflows/.../artifacts/...`.

## Acceptance

- [ ] First green CI run publishes a tarpaulin report
- [ ] PR that drops coverage below 90% fails the gate
- [ ] Report diffable across commits (operators see which lines stopped being covered)

title: P9.2 Integration test harness: docker-compose with 3 Meilisearch nodes + Miroir
description:
## What

Build `examples/docker-compose-dev.yml` + `examples/dev-config.yaml` + `tests/integration/`:

- 3 Meilisearch nodes (getmeili/meilisearch:v1.37.0) on a shared network
- 1 Miroir pod pointing at them via the dev config (RG=1, RF=1, S=16)
- `tests/integration/` with `cargo test --test integration -- --test-threads=1` running against the stack

## Why

Plan §8 "Integration tests" + §11 onboarding: the docker-compose file doubles as the "quick start for a contributor" stack. It's both the test harness and the developer env.

## Details

**docker-compose-dev.yml**:
```yaml
services:
  meili-0: {image: getmeili/meilisearch:v1.37.0, environment: {MEILI_MASTER_KEY: dev-key}}
  meili-1: {same}
  meili-2: {same}
  miroir:  {image: ghcr.io/jedarden/miroir:latest, configmap: dev-config.yaml, ports: [7700, 9090], depends_on: [meili-0, meili-1, meili-2]}
```

**Integration test cases** (plan §8):
- Document round-trip (1000 docs)
- Search covers all shards (unique-keyword test)
- Facet aggregation (3 colors, sum = 100)
- Offset/limit paging
- Settings broadcast
- Task polling
- Node failure with RF=2 — `docker stop meili-1` mid-test

**Test harness utilities**:
- `TestCluster` struct wrapping compose up/down
- Helpers for doc generation, search, stats

## Acceptance

- [ ] `docker-compose up -d` launches a working Miroir-on-3-Meilisearch stack in < 60s
- [ ] `cargo test --test integration -- --test-threads=1` passes all plan §8 integration scenarios
- [ ] Tests clean up after themselves (indexes deleted, compose torn down on Drop)

title: P9.3 API compatibility suite + SDK smoke tests (Py/JS/Go/Rust)
description:
## What

Plan §8 "API compatibility tests":
- Run the same scenarios against a real single-node Meilisearch AND a Miroir instance
- Assert semantic equivalence: same documents retrievable, same search results, same error codes/shapes
- Every Meilisearch error code from plan §5 table verified byte-identical

Plus `examples/sdk-tests/` in **Python, JavaScript, Go, Rust** (plan §8):
- Create index
- Index documents
- Search + verify results
- Update settings
- Delete index

Must pass against **both** docker-compose-dev.yml (Miroir) and a plain Meilisearch instance.

## Why

Plan §1 principle 1 (invisible federation). If Miroir isn't drop-in, the entire value proposition fails. SDK smoke tests prove it empirically in the four most common client languages.

## Details

**Compatibility cases**:
- `POST /indexes` with minimal + maximal body shapes
- `POST /indexes/{uid}/documents` with CSV, NDJSON, JSON arrays
- All search parameters (limit, offset, filter, facets, sort, attributesToRetrieve, ...)
- Error responses for every invalid shape (missing PK, invalid filter, nonexistent index, ...)
- Task lifecycle (enqueue → processing → succeeded/failed; poll and retrieve)

**Error parity harness**:
```rust
#[test]
fn error_parity() {
    for error_case in ERROR_CASES {
        let meili_response = meili_client.call(error_case);
        let miroir_response = miroir_client.call(error_case);
        assert_eq_ignoring_node_ids!(meili_response, miroir_response);
    }
}
```

**SDK tests** live in `examples/sdk-tests/{python,javascript,go,rust}/`. Each is self-contained with its own package/dep management (requirements.txt, package.json, go.mod, Cargo.toml).

## Acceptance

- [ ] 100% of Meilisearch error codes listed in plan §5 produce byte-identical error JSON from Miroir
- [ ] 4/4 SDK smoke tests pass against both Meilisearch and Miroir endpoints
- [ ] Differences (e.g., `X-Miroir-Degraded` header present on Miroir but not Meilisearch) are documented and intentional; never the error body or HTTP status

title: P9.3.a API compatibility test harness + error code parity
description:
## Scope (plan §8)
- Test harness running same scenarios against single-node Meilisearch + Miroir
- Assert semantic equivalence: same docs retrievable, same search results, same error codes/shapes
- Every Meilisearch error code from plan §5 verified byte-identical in error JSON
- No SDKs in this bead — just the Rust-level test harness with HTTP clients

## Acceptance
- [ ] Parameterized test runner takes `(backend_url, scenario)` tuples
- [ ] 100% of Meilisearch error codes produce byte-identical JSON from Miroir
- [ ] Semantic equivalence for: create index, index docs, search, settings update, delete

title: P9.3.b Python SDK smoke test
description: examples/sdk-tests/python/ — create/index/search/settings/delete round-trip using the official meilisearch-python SDK pointed at both docker-compose-dev and plain Meilisearch. Fail on any non-equivalent behavior.

title: P9.3.c JavaScript/TypeScript SDK smoke test
description: examples/sdk-tests/javascript/ — meilisearch-js SDK round-trip (create/index/search/settings/delete) against both backends. package.json + jest/vitest.

title: P9.3.d Go SDK smoke test
description: examples/sdk-tests/go/ — meilisearch-go SDK round-trip against both backends. go.mod + testing package.

title: P9.3.e Rust SDK smoke test
description: examples/sdk-tests/rust/ — meilisearch-sdk crate round-trip against both backends. Cargo.toml; shares test fixtures with miroir-core if useful.

title: P9.4 Chaos test scenarios (tests/chaos/) + runbooks
description:
## What

Plan §8 chaos scenarios, each as a scripted test + a runbook in `tests/chaos/`:

| # | Scenario | Expected result |
|---|----------|-----------------|
| 1 | Kill 1 of 3 nodes (RF=2) | Continuous search; degraded writes warn via header |
| 2 | Kill 2 of 3 nodes (RF=2) | Shard loss; 503 or partial per policy |
| 3 | Kill 1 of 2 Miroir replicas | Zero client-visible downtime |
| 4 | `tc netem delay 500ms` on one node | Searches slow by at most max shard latency; no errors |
| 5 | Restart a killed node | Miroir detects recovery within health check interval, resumes routing |
| 6 | Kill a node mid-rebalance | Rebalancer pauses, resumes on recovery; no data loss |

## Why

Plan §1 principle 5 (graceful degradation). These are the scenarios that convince operators Miroir is production-grade. Each one's expected result matters more than the test itself — the runbook captures what operators should expect during real outages.

## Details

**Test harness**: extend P9.2's `TestCluster` with chaos helpers:
- `cluster.kill_meili(i: usize)` — `docker stop` a node
- `cluster.restart_meili(i)`
- `cluster.apply_netem(i, delay_ms)` — add latency via `tc netem`
- `cluster.kill_miroir()` — scale `miroir` service down then up

**Execution**: these are slow tests (30+ seconds each for recovery cycles). Mark with `#[ignore]` or behind a `--ignored` flag so they don't run in the default `cargo test`. CI runs them on the `miroir-chaos` WorkflowTemplate.

**Runbooks**: `tests/chaos/runbook-<scenario>.md` documents:
- Precondition check
- Manual repro steps
- Expected observable (metrics, headers, client error shape)
- Recovery procedure (if needed)
- How this differs on HA (2+ Miroir replicas)

## Acceptance

- [ ] All 6 scenarios have automated tests passing in the chaos CI run
- [ ] Each has a runbook in `tests/chaos/` reviewed for operator clarity
- [ ] A post-incident reader can use a runbook to confirm whether a given observation was expected

title: P9.4.a Chaos: kill 1 of 3 nodes (RF=2) — continuous search + degraded-write header
description: tests/chaos/scenario-01-kill-one-node.sh + runbook. `docker stop meili-1` mid-traffic. Expected: search continues, writes stamp X-Miroir-Degraded. No client-visible errors beyond retry budget.

title: P9.4.b Chaos: kill 2 of 3 nodes (RF=2) — shard loss, 503 or partial per policy
description: tests/chaos/scenario-02-kill-two-nodes.sh. `docker stop meili-0 meili-1`. Expected: shard loss; unavailable_shard_policy=partial returns X-Miroir-Degraded+hits; =error returns 503 miroir_shard_unavailable.

title: P9.4.c Chaos: kill 1 of 2 Miroir replicas — zero client downtime
description: tests/chaos/scenario-03-kill-miroir-replica.sh. Scale Miroir service down 2→1→2 under load. Expected: Service round-robin routes to survivor; zero client-visible failures (modulo retry budget).

title: P9.4.d Chaos: tc netem delay 500ms on one node — bounded tail latency
description: tests/chaos/scenario-04-netem-delay.sh. Apply tc netem delay 500ms to meili-1; issue 1000 searches. Expected: p95 bounded by slowest shard; no errors. §13.2 hedging (when enabled) should mitigate further.

title: P9.4.e Chaos: restart killed node — recovery detection + routing resume
description: tests/chaos/scenario-05-restart-node.sh. Kill then restart meili-1; confirm Miroir detects recovery within health.interval_ms × recovery_threshold and resumes routing writes to it.

title: P9.4.f Chaos: kill node mid-rebalance — pause, resume, no data loss
description: tests/chaos/scenario-06-kill-during-rebalance.sh. Start node addition (POST /_miroir/nodes); kill source node mid-migration; restart. Expected: rebalancer pauses, resumes from persisted cursor, zero doc loss (Mode B leader + Phase 4 dual-write).

title: P9.5 Performance benches (criterion) + regression gate
description:
## What

Plan §8 "Performance benchmarks" at `benches/` using criterion:

| Benchmark | Target |
|-----------|--------|
| Rendezvous (64 shards, 3 nodes, 10K docs) | < 1 ms total |
| Merger (1000 hits, 3 shards) | < 1 ms |
| End-to-end search latency vs. single-node | < 2× single-node |
| Ingest throughput (1000 docs through Miroir) | > 80% single-node |

Plus a CI bot that comments on any PR increasing measured search latency by > 20% over the previous release.

## Why

Plan §8: "A PR that increases measured search latency by > 20% over the previous release triggers a review comment." Without a regression gate, performance drifts. With it, drift is noticed at the PR level.

## Details

**criterion output artifact**: `target/criterion/` HTML reports; CI uploads as artifact.

**Delta computation**: compare current PR's bench output vs. the most recent `main` run's stored bench output. `critcmp` is the typical tool.

**Gating vs. commenting**: plan §8 says "review comment," not "block merge." Keep the tool advisory — operators trigger reruns for transient noise.

**End-to-end search latency bench** needs a running docker-compose stack; run as part of integration benches, not unit benches.

## Acceptance

- [ ] `cargo bench -p miroir-core` runs in CI and records timings
- [ ] Rendezvous bench passes `< 1 ms` target on iad-ci hardware
- [ ] Merger bench passes `< 1 ms` target
- [ ] End-to-end `< 2×` and ingest `> 80%` verified on a 3-node docker-compose
- [ ] PR with intentional 30% slowdown triggers the comment bot

title: P9.6 Property tests + fuzz for router + config + parser
description:
## What

Add proptest + cargo-fuzz coverage for the critical invariants:

**Router** (`proptest`, in addition to P1.6):
- Given random `(N, RG, RF, S)` and random doc IDs, `write_targets` + `covering_set` satisfy:
  - `|write_targets| == RG × RF` (counting duplicates)
  - Every group has exactly `RF` entries
  - `covering_set` unions to cover every shard in the chosen group
  - Reshuffle on topology change ≤ theoretical optimum

**Config parser**: fuzz `Config::from_yaml` — every valid YAML in the plan parses; adversarial inputs don't crash.

**Filter DSL parser** (§13.4): fuzz the filter grammar — every Meilisearch valid filter parses; malformed filters return `Err`, not panic.

**Canonical-JSON** (for settings hashing §13.5): two equivalent JSONs must hash identically.

## Why

Plan §8 lists property tests in the "Router correctness" section. Adding fuzz to parsers closes the class-of-errors where a single crafted input OOMs or panics the orchestrator.

## Details

**Proptest configs**: 1024 cases per property by default; 8192 in the nightly CI run.

**cargo-fuzz targets** (in `fuzz/fuzz_targets/`):
- `config_parser.rs` — feeds random UTF-8 to `Config::from_yaml_str`
- `filter_parser.rs` — feeds random strings to the §13.4 filter grammar
- `canonical_json.rs` — roundtrips random JSON through the canonicalizer

**Corpus seeding**: include every plan-referenced valid config, filter, and settings block as seeds so fuzz discovers edge cases rather than rediscovering syntax.

## Acceptance

- [ ] `cargo test` runs all property tests at 1024 cases; no rejects
- [ ] `cargo +nightly fuzz run config_parser -- -max_total_time=60` finds no panics in 60s
- [ ] Weekly CI fuzz run (scheduled via Argo Workflow) uploads artifacts showing 0 new crashes

title: Phase 2 — Proxy + API Surface (HTTP routes, quorum, errors)
description:
## Phase 2 Epic — Proxy + API Surface

Wires the Phase 1 primitives into a live HTTP proxy. After this phase, a client pointing a Meilisearch SDK at `http://miroir:7700` can CRUD indexes, write documents, search, and poll tasks — with documents actually sharded across nodes.

## Why This Sits Here

Plan §1 principle 1 (**invisible federation**) and plan §5 (**API Surface and Compatibility**) are the product. Phase 1 gave us math; this phase turns the math into behavior a Meilisearch client sees as drop-in. Every downstream phase assumes these HTTP surfaces exist and return shapes that match the Meilisearch spec exactly, so §8 "API compatibility tests" can pin the contract from here on.

## Scope (plan §3 Lifecycle + §5 API Surface)

- `axum` server listening on `server.port` (default 7700) and metrics on 9090
- **Write path** (plan §2 write path) — hash primary key, inject `_miroir_shard`, fan out to `RG × RF` nodes, per-group quorum (`floor(RF/2)+1`), `X-Miroir-Degraded` on any group missing quorum, 503 `miroir_no_quorum` only when no group met quorum for a shard
- **Read path** (plan §2 read path) — pick group via `query_seq % RG`, build intra-group covering set, scatter, merge by `_rankingScore`, strip `_miroir_shard` always + `_rankingScore` if client didn't request, aggregate facets + estimatedTotalHits, report max processingTimeMs, group-fallback when a covering set has holes
- **Index lifecycle** (plan §3) — create broadcasts + atomically injects `_miroir_shard` into `filterableAttributes`; settings sequential apply-with-rollback (§3 legacy; §13.5 replaces in Phase 5); delete broadcasts; stats aggregate `numberOfDocuments` + merge `fieldDistribution`
- **Tasks** — per plan §3 task ID reconciliation; `GET /tasks`, `GET /tasks/{uid}`, `DELETE /tasks/{uid}`
- **Error shape** — every error matches Meilisearch `{message,code,type,link}`; new `miroir_*` codes per plan §5
- **Reserved fields contract** — `_miroir_shard` always-reserved; `_miroir_updated_at` / `_miroir_expires_at` reserved only when their feature flag is on (Phase 5)
- **Auth** — master-key/admin-key bearer dispatch per §5 "Bearer token dispatch" rules 2–5; JWT path stubbed (Phase 5)
- **/health + /version + /_miroir/ready + /_miroir/topology + /_miroir/shards** + **/_miroir/metrics** (admin-key gated mirror of port 9090 /metrics per plan §10)
- **Middleware** — structured JSON log per plan §10; Prometheus metrics (`miroir_request_duration_seconds`, etc.)
- **Scatter-gather dispatcher** — per-node retries with orchestrator-side retry cache keyed by `sha256(batch || target_node || idempotency_or_mtask)` (plan §4 note on `scatter.retry_on_timeout`)

## Out of Scope (moved to later phases)

- Two-phase settings broadcast (→ Phase 5 / §13.5)
- Persistent task store (→ Phase 3)
- Rebalancer (→ Phase 4)
- Any §13 feature (→ Phase 5)
- Multi-replica coordination / Redis / HPA (→ Phase 6)

## Definition of Done

- [ ] Integration test: 1000 documents indexed across 3 nodes, each retrievable by ID (plan §8)
- [ ] Integration test: unique-keyword search finds every doc exactly once (plan §8)
- [ ] Integration test: facet aggregation across 3 color values sums correctly (plan §8)
- [ ] Integration test: offset/limit paging preserves global ordering (plan §8)
- [ ] Integration test: write with one group completely down still succeeds on remaining group and stamps `X-Miroir-Degraded`
- [ ] Error-format parity test: every `invalid_request`/`not_found`/`document_*` code matches Meilisearch output byte-for-byte on equivalent input
- [ ] `GET /_miroir/topology` matches the shape in plan §10
close_reason: done

title: P2.1 axum server skeleton + config loader + /health + /version + /_miroir/ready
description:
## What

Flesh out `miroir-proxy::main`:
- Load `Config` (file + env + CLI args overlay)
- Initialize tracing (JSON-to-stdout per plan §10 log format)
- Start two axum listeners: `:7700` (client API) + `:9090` (metrics, unauthenticated, pod-internal)
- Signal handlers for graceful shutdown (SIGTERM → stop accepting new requests → drain in-flight → exit)
- Implement: `GET /health`, `GET /version`, `GET /_miroir/ready`, `GET /_miroir/topology`, `GET /_miroir/shards`, `GET /_miroir/metrics`

## Why

These are the minimum-viable endpoints Kubernetes needs to probe and operators need to inspect. `GET /health` is Meilisearch-compatible — the K8s liveness probe — and must return 200 immediately regardless of internal state (Meilisearch semantics). `GET /_miroir/ready` is the readiness probe and *blocks* 503 until a covering quorum is reachable on first startup (plan §10).

## Details

**`/health`** (plan §10) — returns `{"status":"available"}`. Never gate on internal state.

**`/version`** — per plan §5 "Orchestrator-local": return the Meilisearch version from any healthy node. Cache at ~60s TTL.

**`/_miroir/ready`** — 503 during startup; 200 once Miroir has loaded config + verified a covering quorum of nodes is reachable. This is specifically where the "there's at least one full covering set somewhere in the topology" check lives.

**`/_miroir/topology`** — shape exactly per plan §10 JSON sample: `shards`, `replication_factor`, `nodes[]` with `id/status/shard_count/last_seen_ms[/error]`, `degraded_node_count`, `rebalance_in_progress`, `fully_covered`.

**`/_miroir/shards`** — shard → node mapping table for the current topology (useful for runbooks and for §13.20 explain).

**`/_miroir/metrics`** — admin-key-gated mirror of port 9090 `/metrics`. Same data; admin-authenticated so it can be exposed outside the cluster.

## Acceptance

- [ ] `curl localhost:7700/health` returns 200 within 100ms of process start
- [ ] `curl localhost:7700/_miroir/ready` returns 503 until all configured nodes are reachable, then 200
- [ ] `curl -H "Authorization: Bearer $ADMIN_KEY" localhost:7700/_miroir/topology | jq .` matches the plan §10 shape
- [ ] SIGTERM drains in-flight requests (test by sending signal during a long-running search)
close_reason: done

title: P2.2 Document write path: primary key → hash → shard → fan-out → quorum
description:
## What

Implement:
- `POST /indexes/{uid}/documents`
- `PUT /indexes/{uid}/documents`
- `DELETE /indexes/{uid}/documents/{id}`
- `DELETE /indexes/{uid}/documents` (by IDs array or filter)

## Why

Plan §2 "Write path" is the heart of the product. Four properties that MUST be right:

1. **Primary key extraction on the hot path** — plan §3 "Primary key requirement" says batches without a resolvable primary key are rejected before touching any node. This is a cheap, up-front check and a big UX win.
2. **`_miroir_shard` injection** (plan §2 "Inject `_miroir_shard`") — every document gets `_miroir_shard: shard_id` added before forwarding. Stored as a filterable attribute (set at index creation), used by Phase 4 rebalancer and Phase 5 §13.8 anti-entropy for targeted shard retrieval. Stripped from all API responses.
3. **Rejection of `_miroir_shard` in client-submitted docs** — plan §2 "`_miroir_shard` is a reserved field name": 400 `miroir_reserved_field` if present on the inbound doc.
4. **Two-rule quorum** (plan §2):
   - Per-group quorum = `floor(RF/2) + 1` ACKs from that group's RF nodes
   - Write success if ≥ 1 group met its per-group quorum; `X-Miroir-Degraded` header if ANY group missed
   - HTTP 503 `miroir_no_quorum` only if NO group met its per-group quorum for a given shard

## Details

**Per-batch grouping** (plan §3 "Ingest (add/replace)"): group documents by target node set so each node gets exactly one HTTP request containing all the docs it owns. This minimizes HTTP fan-out count (critical at scale).

**Retry-on-timeout** (plan §4 "Note on `scatter.retry_on_timeout`"): orchestrator-side retry cache keyed by `sha256(batch || target_node || idempotency_key_or_mtask_id)`. When a timeout retries, check the cache first; if the prior dispatch has a cached terminal response, return it rather than creating a duplicate node-side task.

**Delete-by-filter** (plan §5 "Broadcast to all nodes"): cannot be shard-routed; broadcast to every node.

**Delete-by-IDs array**: route each ID to its shard independently (same routing as the write path).

## Acceptance (plan §8)

- [ ] 1000 docs indexed via POST — every doc fetch-by-id returns the same doc
- [ ] Docs distribute across all configured nodes (no node holds < 20% under RF=1/3-node)
- [ ] Batch with one missing primary key → 400 `miroir_primary_key_required`, no docs written anywhere
- [ ] Doc containing `_miroir_shard` → 400 `miroir_reserved_field`
- [ ] RG=2, RF=1, 1 group down: write to 1 group succeeds with `X-Miroir-Degraded: groups=1`
- [ ] RG=2, RF=1, both groups down: 503 `miroir_no_quorum`
- [ ] DELETE by IDs array [docA, docB] with docA on shard 3, docB on shard 7 produces 2 independent per-shard delete calls
close_reason: Implemented P2.2 write path with POST/PUT/DELETE document endpoints. Primary key validation, _miroir_shard injection, reserved field rejection, two-rule quorum, degraded header support. 15 acceptance tests pass.

title: P2.3 Search read path: scatter-gather + merge + group selection
description:
## What

Implement `POST /indexes/{uid}/search`:
1. Pick group = `query_seq % RG` (plan §2)
2. Build intra-group covering set (plan §4 `covering_set`)
3. Fan out search to each node in covering set **with `showRankingScore: true` appended** (plan §2 read path step 4)
4. Each node must return up to `offset + limit` results (plan §2 read path "offset/limit")
5. Use P1.4 `merge` to collapse shard hits → single response

## Why

Read latency == max shard latency. This is where hedging (§13.2), adaptive replica selection (§13.3), and query coalescing (§13.10) will plug in during Phase 5 — so the routing decisions need to be factored cleanly into a `ScatterPlan` now rather than hard-wired.

## Details

**`showRankingScore: true` is injected unconditionally** so the merger can global-sort. After merging, the response strips `_rankingScore` unless the client originally asked for it.

**Partial unavailability** (plan §3 `unavailable_shard_policy: partial`, default): if a shard is fully unavailable, return best-effort hits with `X-Miroir-Degraded: shards=3,7,11`. `unavailable_shard_policy: error` instead returns 503 + `miroir_shard_unavailable`.

**Group-unavailability fallback** (plan §2 "Group unavailability fallback"): if the selected group has a shard with no available intra-group RF replica, Miroir optionally falls back to a different group for **that query** (full result, different group).

**Facets** — plan §2 step 7: sum per-value counts across the covering set.

**`estimatedTotalHits`** — sum across covering set.

**`processingTimeMs`** — max across covering set.

## Acceptance (plan §8)

- [ ] Unique-keyword search across 3 nodes returns exactly 1 hit (proves merger + fan-out correctness)
- [ ] Facet counts sum correctly across shards
- [ ] Paging: 5 pages of 10 = single limit=50 order, no dupes/gaps
- [ ] With one node down and RF=2: search still covers all shards (tests fall-back within the group)
- [ ] With one group fully down: search uses the other group; response is not `X-Miroir-Degraded`
- [ ] `X-Miroir-Degraded: shards=...` stamped when a shard has zero live replicas
close_reason:
P2.3 Search read path complete with all acceptance tests passing:

**Implemented:**
- POST /indexes/{uid}/search with scatter-gather + merge + group selection
- Group selection via query_seq % RG (round-robin across replica groups)
- Intra-group covering set using plan §4 covering_set
- Fan out to all nodes in covering set with showRankingScore: true injected unconditionally
- Each node returns offset + limit results for coordinator pagination
- P1.4 merge (score-based with global IDF, RRF fallback)
- X-Miroir-Degraded: shards=X,Y,Z header for partial unavailability
- Group-unavailability fallback (Fallback policy)
- Facet count aggregation (sum across covering set)
- estimatedTotalHits = sum across covering set
- processingTimeMs = max across covering set

**Acceptance tests passing (10/10):**
- Unique-keyword search across 3 nodes returns exactly 1 hit (proves merger + fan-out correctness)
- Facet counts sum correctly across shards
- Paging: 5 pages of 10 = single limit=50 order, no dupes/gaps
- With one node down and RF=2: search still covers all shards (intra-group fallback)
- With one group fully down: search uses the other group; response is not X-Miroir-Degraded
- X-Miroir-Degraded: shards=... stamped when a shard has zero live replicas

**Technical details:**
- SearchRequest.to_node_body() injects showRankingScore: true unconditionally
- Coordinator applies offset/limit after global merge (nodes receive offset=0, limit=offset+limit)
- _rankingScore stripped unless client originally requested it
- ScoreMergeStrategy for global-IDF mode (OP#4), RrfStrategy as fallback
- Preflight phase aggregates global IDF for cross-shard score comparability

title: P2.4 Index lifecycle endpoints: create/update/delete + settings broadcast
description:
## What

Implement:
- `POST /indexes` — create index; broadcast to every node; atomically adds `_miroir_shard` to `filterableAttributes`
- `PATCH /indexes/{uid}` — settings updates; sequential apply-with-rollback (legacy strategy; §13.5 two-phase broadcast replaces in Phase 5)
- `DELETE /indexes/{uid}` — broadcast
- `GET /indexes/{uid}/stats` + `GET /stats` — fan out, sum `numberOfDocuments`, merge `fieldDistribution`
- `POST /keys`, `PATCH /keys/{key}`, `DELETE /keys/{key}` — broadcast

## Why

**Plan §3 "Index lifecycle"**: create must broadcast, every node creates the same index with the same settings. Partial creation is rolled back. Plan explicitly calls this "the highest-risk operation in the lifecycle" — the motivation for §13.5. For Phase 2, ship the legacy sequential-with-rollback path (it's what plan §3 describes before §13.5).

**Crucial subtlety**: plan §3 says index creation "additionally broadcasts a settings update to add `_miroir_shard` to `filterableAttributes` on every node — this is required for efficient rebalancing." This is not optional — Phase 4's rebalancer relies on it, and there's no way to add it after the fact without full reindex.

## Details

**Create rollback**: if any node fails, `DELETE /indexes/{uid}` on all previously-created nodes. The final error surfaces to the client with sufficient detail to diagnose which node failed.

**Settings sequential**:
1. Apply to node-0, verify via `GET /indexes/{uid}/settings`
2. Apply to node-1, verify
3. ... all nodes
4. On failure: revert all previously applied nodes to the pre-change settings snapshot

**Settings bucket under `__reserved_settings` for §13.5 verify** — capture the exact bytes of current settings before every PATCH so rollback is lossless.

**Delete-by-filter** — broadcast; note that this is a document endpoint, but the code path joins here.

**Stats aggregation**:
- `numberOfDocuments` — sum across all nodes (duplicates per-replica across RG×RF; divide by (RG × RF) to get logical doc count)
- `fieldDistribution` — sum per-field counts across nodes

## Acceptance

- [ ] `POST /indexes` creates an index on every node; failure on any node rolls back
- [ ] Settings broadcast sequential: a mid-broadcast node failure reverts all previously applied nodes
- [ ] `_miroir_shard` is in `filterableAttributes` immediately after index creation (verified via `GET /indexes/{uid}/settings`)
- [ ] `GET /indexes/{uid}/stats` `numberOfDocuments` = logical count (not replica-multiplied)
- [ ] `/keys` CRUD broadcasts; all-or-nothing (atomic across nodes)
close_reason: P2.4 index lifecycle endpoints complete. POST /indexes broadcasts with rollback + _miroir_shard. PATCH/DELETE broadcast. Stats fan-out with logical doc count. Keys CRUD all-or-nothing. 11 p24 tests + 69 workspace tests passing.

title: P2.5 Task ID reconciliation and /tasks endpoints
description:
## What

Implement plan §3 "Task ID reconciliation":
- Every write fan-out collects per-node `taskUid` values
- Generate a Miroir task ID `mtask-<uuid>`
- Persist `mtask → {node_id: node_task_uid}` in the in-memory task registry (Phase 3 makes it durable)
- Return `mtask-xxxxx` to client as `{"taskUid": ...}` in Meilisearch shape
- `GET /tasks/{mtask_id}` polls every mapped node task, aggregates:
  - `succeeded` — all nodes report `succeeded`
  - `failed` — any node reports `failed`; include the per-node error detail
  - `processing` — otherwise
- `GET /tasks?statuses=...` — list across all mtasks with Meilisearch-compatible query params

## Why

Clients (SDKs) use the Meilisearch task API as-is. Not reconciling = clients see a single success event but writes have only partially landed (durability bug). Conversely, reconciling too eagerly (polling every ms) blows CPU and node load for nothing.

## Details

**Polling cadence**: exponential backoff per mtask: 25 ms → 50 → 100 → ... cap at 1s. Stop polling once terminal.

**Retention**: default 7 days, pruned by Mode A rendezvous-partitioned pruner (Phase 6 §14.5). Until Phase 3, retention is in-memory only.

**Error aggregation**: if any node fails, present a compact Meilisearch-shaped error but include per-node breakdown as `error.details`.

**`GET /tasks`** (Meilisearch-compatible filters): `statuses`, `types`, `indexUids`, `from`, `limit`. Must paginate across mtasks consistently.

**`DELETE /tasks/{mtask_id}`** — cancel if possible (delegate to Meilisearch; may no-op if Meilisearch doesn't support cancel on that type).

## Acceptance

- [ ] Fan-out to 3 nodes → all 3 `taskUid`s captured in one mtask
- [ ] `GET /tasks/{mtask_id}` while all nodes are processing → `processing`
- [ ] One node fails → status `failed`, error includes per-node breakdown
- [ ] In-memory registry survives the request's own lifetime (Phase 3 makes it persistent)
close_reason:
Implemented P2.5 task ID reconciliation and /tasks endpoints:
- InMemoryTaskRegistry with mtask-<uuid> generation
- Write path collects per-node taskUid values and registers unified mtask
- GET /tasks/{id} polls nodes and aggregates status (succeeded/failed/processing)
- GET /tasks with Meilisearch-compatible filters (statuses, types, indexUids, from, limit)
- DELETE /tasks/{id} for best-effort cancellation
- Exponential backoff polling (25ms → 50 → 100 → ... → 1s cap)
- Per-node error breakdown in failed task responses
- NodeClient.get_task_status() for polling individual node tasks

title: P2.6 Error mapping and Meilisearch-compatible error shape
description:
## What

Implement the error response shape from plan §5:
```json
{"message": "...", "code": "...", "type": "invalid_request", "link": "..."}
```

And every `miroir_*` code from plan §5:
- `miroir_primary_key_required`
- `miroir_no_quorum`
- `miroir_shard_unavailable`
- `miroir_reserved_field` (covers `_miroir_shard` always; `_miroir_updated_at` + `_miroir_expires_at` only when their feature flags are on)
- `miroir_idempotency_key_reused` (Phase 5 §13.10)
- `miroir_settings_version_stale` (Phase 5 §13.5)
- `miroir_multi_alias_not_writable` (Phase 5 §13.7)
- `miroir_jwt_invalid` (Phase 5 §13.21)
- `miroir_jwt_scope_denied` (Phase 5 §13.21)
- `miroir_invalid_auth`

Plus: forward Meilisearch errors verbatim when the failure happened node-side.

## Why

Plan §8 API compatibility: "Test every expected Meilisearch error code against both real Meilisearch and Miroir." The shape and code vocabulary must match so existing SDKs' error handling branches stay functional. Custom codes live under a disjoint `miroir_` prefix so a client's "unknown error" branch handles them safely.

## Details

**Error type enum**: `invalid_request`, `auth`, `internal`, `system` — mirroring Meilisearch categories. Each `miroir_*` code maps to one of these.

**Link field**: point at `https://github.com/jedarden/miroir/blob/main/docs/errors.md#<code>` — anchors generated at build time.

**Error struct**:
```rust
#[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error, serde::Serialize)]
pub struct MeilisearchError {
    pub message: String,
    pub code: String,   // e.g. "miroir_no_quorum" or "document_not_found"
    #[serde(rename = "type")]
    pub error_type: ErrorType,
    pub link: Option<String>,
}
```

**Status codes**:
- 400: primary_key_required, reserved_field
- 401: invalid_auth, jwt_invalid
- 403: jwt_scope_denied
- 409: idempotency_key_reused, multi_alias_not_writable
- 503: no_quorum, shard_unavailable, settings_version_stale

## Acceptance

- [ ] Every code in plan §5 table has a unit test producing the expected JSON shape
- [ ] Meilisearch-native error passes through unchanged (forwarded from node responses)
- [ ] HTTP status codes match the plan §5 mapping
close_reason: P2.6 complete. All acceptance criteria met: (1) 10 per-code JSON shape tests, (2) Meilisearch-native error forwarding via forwarded() with round-trip tests, (3) HTTP status code mapping verified. Commits: 9606af8 (core shape + tests), fca081e (proxy integration).

title: P2.7 Auth: bearer-token dispatch (plan §5 rules 0-5) + X-Admin-Key
description:
## What

Implement the bearer-token dispatch chain from plan §5 "Bearer token dispatch":

0. **Dispatch-exempt check** — if (method, path) is in the exempt list, run handler directly
1. **JWT-shape probe** — if token parses as JWT, validate as search-UI JWT (signature, exp/nbf, kid, idx, scope). Parseable-but-invalid → 401 `miroir_jwt_invalid`. Signature-valid but scope mismatch → 403 `miroir_jwt_scope_denied`. Phase 5 §13.21 adds the JWT validation; Phase 2 stubs this to "not-a-jwt → next step"
2. **Admin-path opaque-token match** — path starts with `/_miroir/`, match against `admin_key`. Exempt: `/_miroir/metrics`, `/_miroir/ui/search/locale/*`, `POST /_miroir/admin/login`, `GET /_miroir/ui/search/{index}/session`
3. **Master-key match** — other paths → `master_key`
4. **Mismatch** → 401 `miroir_invalid_auth`
5. **Dispatch-exempt endpoints** — exhaustive list in plan §5 rule 5

Plus: `X-Admin-Key` short-circuit for admin endpoints.

## Why

Plan §5: "Three token types can appear on `Authorization: Bearer <value>` simultaneously — the `master_key`, the `admin_key`, and a search UI JWT. Miroir resolves them deterministically." Without a consistent dispatch chain, Phase 5 §13.21's JWT path conflicts with admin/master key on the same header. Getting it deterministic now means Phase 5 just slots JWT validation in at rule 1.

## Details

**Rule 0 list** (needs to be kept in sync with §5 table 5):
- `GET /_miroir/metrics` — admin-key-optional
- `GET /_miroir/ui/search/locale/*` — unauthenticated
- `POST /_miroir/admin/login` — credentials in body
- `GET /_miroir/ui/search/{index}/session` — auth per `search_ui.auth.mode`
- `GET /ui/search/{index}` — public SPA

**Constant-time comparison**: use `subtle::ConstantTimeEq` for all opaque-token comparisons to prevent timing side-channels.

**Rate-limit hooks**: wire in `miroir:ratelimit:adminlogin:<ip>` and `miroir:ratelimit:searchui:<ip>` bucket counters from Phase 3 task store; Phase 2 may keep in-memory until Phase 6 multi-pod.

## Acceptance

- [ ] Every row in plan §5 rule 5 exempt list has a unit test (request does NOT match admin_key / master_key)
- [ ] Opaque token on `/_miroir/*` matches only admin_key; never master_key
- [ ] Opaque token on other paths matches only master_key; never admin_key
- [ ] Missing Authorization on auth-gated endpoints → 401 `miroir_invalid_auth`
- [ ] `X-Admin-Key` alone gates admin endpoints equivalently to Bearer admin_key
- [ ] Constant-time compare: test with timing-injection harness shows no measurable delta between "wrong length" and "wrong bytes"
close_reason: P2.7 Auth bearer-token dispatch complete. All plan S5 rules 0-5 implemented in auth.rs (819 lines, 51 unit tests). All acceptance criteria met. Already committed in 625e414.

title: P2.8 Middleware: structured logging + prometheus metrics + request IDs
description:
## What

Implement `miroir-proxy::middleware`:
- Request ID generation (UUIDv7 prefix short-hashed) attached as `X-Request-Id` on every response
- Structured JSON log per plan §10 shape (timestamp, level, message, index, duration_ms, node_count, estimated_hits, degraded)
- Prometheus histogram: `miroir_request_duration_seconds{method, path_template, status}`
- Counter: `miroir_requests_total{method, path_template, status}`
- Gauge: `miroir_requests_in_flight`
- Scatter metrics: `miroir_scatter_fan_out_size`, `miroir_scatter_partial_responses_total`, `miroir_scatter_retries_total`
- Node metrics: `miroir_node_healthy`, `miroir_node_request_duration_seconds`, `miroir_node_errors_total`

## Why

Phase 7 builds dashboards and alerts on these exact metric names. Defining them here (not at Phase 7) means every P2.X feature already emits the right signals without retrofit.

**`path_template` (not `path`)** is critical: `/indexes/{uid}/search` is a template; substituting actual values produces high-cardinality labels that OOM Prometheus. Axum provides the matched route template via `MatchedPath` extractor.

## Details

**Log format** (plan §10 exact shape):
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-05-01T12:00:00.000Z",
  "level": "info",
  "message": "search completed",
  "index": "products",
  "duration_ms": 42,
  "node_count": 3,
  "estimated_hits": 15420,
  "degraded": false
}
```

Logs go to stdout, one JSON object per line. Use `tracing-subscriber` with `fmt::layer().json()`.

**In-flight gauge**: increment on request start, decrement via `Drop` guard so even panics decrement correctly.

**Metrics server on `:9090`**: separate axum listener from the client API; no auth (bound to cluster network); `/metrics` returns prometheus exposition format.

## Acceptance

- [ ] `curl localhost:9090/metrics` returns all listed metrics with ≥ 1 sample after a single request
- [ ] `jq` parses every log line without error
- [ ] Request ID appears in response header and in the log entry for that request
- [ ] High-cardinality defense: `path_template` never contains a UUID or arbitrary UID
close_reason:
P2.8 Middleware: structured logging + prometheus metrics + request IDs

Implementation already complete in commit fca081e. Verified all acceptance criteria:

- curl localhost:9090/metrics returns all listed metrics with >= 1 sample after a single request
- jq parses every log line without error  
- Request ID appears in response header (x-request-id) and in the log entry for that request
- High-cardinality defense: path_template (e.g. /health, /indexes/{uid}/search) never contains a UUID or arbitrary UID - uses Axum MatchedPath extractor

Metrics implemented:
- miroir_request_duration_seconds{method, path_template, status}
- miroir_requests_total{method, path_template, status}
- miroir_requests_in_flight
- miroir_scatter_fan_out_size
- miroir_scatter_partial_responses_total
- miroir_scatter_retries_total
- miroir_node_healthy
- miroir_node_request_duration_seconds
- miroir_node_errors_total

Request ID generation uses UUIDv7 prefix short-hashed (16 hex chars). Structured JSON logging via tracing-subscriber with JSON formatter.

title: Phase 7 — Observability + Ops (§10)
description:
## Phase 7 Epic — Observability + Ops

Ships the metric set, log format, tracing hooks, alert rules, and Grafana dashboard specified in plan §10 + the resource-pressure additions from §14.9.

## Why A Dedicated Phase

Observability accretes badly: if you wire metrics per-feature, you end up with inconsistent naming, duplicate counters, and missing labels. Plan §10 names every metric up front so Phase 5 can depend on a stable registry. This phase makes sure the registry lines up with the plan and the Grafana dashboard reads real data.

## Scope (plan §10 + §14.9)

**Health endpoints**
- `GET /health` — Meilisearch-compatible, used as liveness
- `GET /_miroir/ready` — readiness; 503 until covering quorum reachable
- `GET /_miroir/topology` — full cluster state (shape in plan §10)

**Prometheus metrics** (all prefixed `miroir_`)
- Requests: `miroir_request_duration_seconds{method,path_template,status}` histogram, `miroir_requests_total` counter, `miroir_requests_in_flight` gauge
- Node health: `miroir_node_healthy{node_id}`, `miroir_node_request_duration_seconds{node_id,operation}`, `miroir_node_errors_total{node_id,error_type}`
- Shards: `miroir_shard_coverage`, `miroir_degraded_shards_total`, `miroir_shard_distribution{node_id}`
- Task registry: `miroir_task_processing_age_seconds`, `miroir_tasks_total{status}`, `miroir_task_registry_size`
- Scatter-gather: `miroir_scatter_fan_out_size`, `miroir_scatter_partial_responses_total`, `miroir_scatter_retries_total`
- Rebalancer: `miroir_rebalance_in_progress`, `miroir_rebalance_documents_migrated_total`, `miroir_rebalance_duration_seconds`
- §13.11–21 family groups (all 11 listed in plan §10 "Advanced capabilities metrics")
- §14.9 resource-pressure: `miroir_memory_pressure`, `miroir_cpu_throttled_seconds_total`, `miroir_request_queue_depth`, `miroir_background_queue_depth{job_type}`, `miroir_peer_pod_count`, `miroir_leader`, `miroir_owned_shards_count`

**Ports**
- Port 7700: `/_miroir/metrics` admin-key-gated
- Port 9090: `/metrics` unauthenticated, pod-internal, ServiceMonitor target

**Grafana dashboard** (`dashboards/miroir-overview.json`) — 8 panels per plan §10 + feature-flag-gated panels for §13.11–21 when flags are on

**ServiceMonitor** (plan §10 YAML)

**Alerting** (`PrometheusRule` per plan §10 + §14.9)
- MiroirDegradedShards, MiroirNodeDown, MiroirHighSearchLatency, MiroirTaskStuck, MiroirRebalanceStuck
- MiroirSettingsDivergence (paired with §13.5 reconciler)
- MiroirAntientropyMismatch (paired with §13.8 at 3 consecutive passes)
- MiroirMemoryPressure, MiroirRequestQueueBacklog, MiroirBackgroundJobBacklog, MiroirPeerDiscoveryGap, MiroirNoLeader

**Tracing (optional)** — OpenTelemetry with configurable sample_rate; disabled by default; each search produces one parent span with a child per covering-set node

**Log format** — structured JSON to stdout; schema per plan §10

## Definition of Done

- [ ] Every metric in plan §10 + §14.9 registered and scraping on port 9090
- [ ] `/_miroir/metrics` on port 7700 returns identical data when admin-key-authenticated
- [ ] Grafana dashboard JSON imports cleanly; all 8 core panels render from a live scrape
- [ ] All 12 alerts live in the shipped PrometheusRule manifest
- [ ] OTel trace contains one parent span per request and one child per node call
- [ ] Log entries match the schema verbatim (parseable as JSON)
- [ ] ServiceMonitor picks up the metrics service in a kind cluster test
close_reason: done

title: P7.1 Core metrics families: requests, nodes, shards, tasks, scatter, rebalancer
description:
## What

Register the plan §10 core metric families on `:9090/metrics` AND `/_miroir/metrics` (admin-key gated mirror):

**Requests** (histogram + counter + gauge):
- `miroir_request_duration_seconds{method, path_template, status}`
- `miroir_requests_total{method, path_template, status}`
- `miroir_requests_in_flight`

**Node health**:
- `miroir_node_healthy{node_id}`
- `miroir_node_request_duration_seconds{node_id, operation}`
- `miroir_node_errors_total{node_id, error_type}`

**Shards**:
- `miroir_shard_coverage`
- `miroir_degraded_shards_total`
- `miroir_shard_distribution{node_id}`

**Tasks**:
- `miroir_task_processing_age_seconds`
- `miroir_tasks_total{status}`
- `miroir_task_registry_size`

**Scatter-gather**:
- `miroir_scatter_fan_out_size`
- `miroir_scatter_partial_responses_total`
- `miroir_scatter_retries_total`

**Rebalancer**:
- `miroir_rebalance_in_progress`
- `miroir_rebalance_documents_migrated_total`
- `miroir_rebalance_duration_seconds`

## Why

Plan §10 + Phase 9 dashboard + alerts all depend on these exact names. Naming is a contract — changing them post-v1.0 breaks every downstream dashboard + alert rule.

## Details

**Label cardinality defense**:
- `path_template` MUST be the axum matched path (not the raw URL)
- `node_id` is bounded (~dozens)
- `status` is the HTTP status code (~10s)
- `error_type` is enum-limited (not a raw error string)
- `operation` is the backend call name ({search, documents_post, stats_get, ...})

**Histogram buckets**: use prometheus default buckets for duration histograms unless the plan calls out specifics.

**Port 9090 (unauth, pod-internal)** is the canonical scrape target; port 7700 `/_miroir/metrics` (admin-auth) returns identical data for ad-hoc inspection from outside.

## Acceptance

- [ ] `curl localhost:9090/metrics | grep '^miroir_'` lists every metric name above
- [ ] `curl -H "Authorization: Bearer $ADMIN_KEY" localhost:7700/_miroir/metrics` returns the same data
- [ ] `path_template` labels contain no UUIDs or dynamic segments
- [ ] A request that hits 3 nodes produces a `miroir_scatter_fan_out_size` histogram sample of 3
close_reason: P7.1 complete: All 18 plan §10 core metric families registered on :9090/metrics and /_miroir/metrics (admin-key gated). Label cardinality bounded (path_template uses axum MatchedPath, no UUIDs). Search handler records scatter_fan_out_size. All 111 tests pass.

title: P7.2 §13.11-21 metric families wired behind feature flags
description:
## What

- Multi-search (§13.11): `miroir_multisearch_queries_per_batch`, `miroir_multisearch_batches_total`, `miroir_multisearch_partial_failures_total`, `miroir_tenant_session_pin_override_total{tenant}`

- Shadow (§13.16): `miroir_shadow_diff_total{kind}`, `miroir_shadow_kendall_tau`, `miroir_shadow_latency_delta_seconds`, `miroir_shadow_errors_total{target,side}`
- ILM (§13.17): `miroir_rollover_events_total{policy}`, `miroir_rollover_active_indexes{alias}`, `miroir_rollover_documents_expired_total{policy}`, `miroir_rollover_last_action_seconds{policy}`
- Canary (§13.18): `miroir_canary_runs_total{canary,result}`, `miroir_canary_latency_ms{canary}`, `miroir_canary_assertion_failures_total{canary,assertion_type}`
