Aliases: list/create/flip/delete with history timeline (§13.7). 2PC preview is the critical feature — shows operators what the §13.5 propose/verify/commit flow will do before they click Apply.

title: P5.19.c Documents + Query Sandbox + Tasks sections
description: Plan §13.19. Documents: paginated browser per index; filter builder; CSV/NDJSON drag-and-drop triggers §13.9 streaming import. Query Sandbox: filter/sort/facet builders; instant-run with per-shard latency breakdown; one-click §13.20 explain; side-by-side diff vs. §13.16 shadow. Tasks: active + recent tasks; per-node breakdown; retry/cancel where applicable.

title: P5.19.3.a Admin UI: Documents section (browser, filter, CSV/NDJSON import)
description: Plan §13.19 Documents section. Paginated document browser per index; filter builder with UI hints; drag-and-drop CSV/NDJSON triggers §13.9 streaming import via POST /_miroir/dumps/import. Show per-row controls (view/edit/delete).

title: P5.19.3.b Admin UI: Query Sandbox with shard-level latency + explain integration
description: Plan §13.19 Query Sandbox. Filter/sort/facet-request builders. Instant-run button with per-shard latency breakdown visible. One-click §13.20 explain invocation rendering the plan visually (shard-to-node arrows, color-coded warnings). Side-by-side diff vs §13.16 shadow when enabled.

title: P5.19.3.c Admin UI: Tasks section (active+recent, per-node breakdown, retry/cancel)
description: Plan §13.19 Tasks section. Table of active + recent tasks (mtask_id, index, status, duration). Expand a row → per-node task breakdown. Retry/cancel controls where applicable (delegates to Meilisearch cancel API).

title: P5.19.d Canaries + Shadow Diff + CDC Inspector + Metrics + Settings sections
description: Plan §13.19. Canaries: list/create/edit/disable; pass-fail heatmap over time; seed-from-traffic flow (§13.18). Shadow Diff: live stream + aggregated summary from §13.16. CDC Inspector: subscribe to live tail of §13.13 with filter by index/operation. Metrics: Grafana iframe OR direct Prometheus panel render. Settings: read/edit Miroir config with restart hints for runtime-vs-reload knobs.

title: P5.19.e Login/logout + CSRF + session seal + rate limit + responsive design
description: Plan §13.19 Admin UI non-section concerns: login form → POST /_miroir/admin/login (session cookie via §9 ADMIN_SESSION_SEAL_KEY). Logout → POST /_miroir/admin/logout (session revoked, Redis Pub/Sub propagation). CSRF double-submit via X-CSRF-Token on state-changing requests. Login rate limit 10/minute per IP + exponential backoff (§10 P10.7). Responsive breakpoints: mobile <640, tablet 640-1024, desktop ≥1024, max-width 1440. WCAG 2.2 AA. Bundle ≤ 100 KB gzipped. Destructive-action confirm modal echoing target name.

title: P5.2 §13.2 Hedged requests for tail-latency mitigation
description:
## What

Implement tail-latency hedging for reads (plan §13.2):
- Each in-flight node request starts a hedge timer at that node's rolling p95 latency (measured by §13.3 EWMA)
- If timer fires, issue duplicate request to another replica (intra-group alternate, or cross-group if policy permits)
- `tokio::select!` races both; loser's future is dropped (aborts Miroir-side HTTP connection)

Applies to reads ONLY — `/search`, `/indexes/{uid}/documents`, `/indexes/{uid}/documents/{id}`. Writes are never hedged (duplicates produce extra Meilisearch tasks + potential auto-ID dupes).

## Why

Plan §13.2: "A scatter-gather query's latency is bounded by the slowest responding shard. A single GC-paused or disk-throttled node poisons p99 across the whole fleet." Hedging trades a small cost (occasional extra node request) for a large win (tail latency roughly halved on skewed workloads).

## Details

**Config** (plan §13.2):
```yaml
hedging:
  enabled: true
  p95_trigger_multiplier: 1.2
  min_trigger_ms: 15
  max_hedges_per_query: 2
  cross_group_fallback: true
```

**Idempotency**: reads are side-effect-free, so no cache needed. Just race.

**Scaling mode**: stateless per-request; each pod hedges its own requests independently.

**Interaction with §13.3**: hedging reads the per-node p95 from the same EWMA registry §13.3 writes to.

## Acceptance

- [ ] Chaos test: `tc netem delay 500ms` on one of 3 nodes; hedged fan-out avoids the slow node via the other 2 replicas; p95 close to healthy-cluster p95
- [ ] Write path verified NOT to hedge (no duplicate node task IDs under any scenario)
- [ ] `miroir_hedge_fired_total{outcome=winner|loser}` counters tick in test runs
- [ ] `max_hedges_per_query` cap prevents thundering herd under widespread node degradation

title: P5.20 §13.20 Query Explain API
description:
## What

`POST /indexes/{uid}/explain` — same body as `/search`, returns the orchestrator's resolved plan without executing (plan §13.20). `?execute=true` also runs the plan and returns the real result.

## Plan shape (plan §13.20 example):

```json
{
  "resolved_uid": "products_v4",
  "plan": {
    "alias_resolution": {"from": "products", "to": "products_v4", "version": 7},
    "narrowed": true,
    "narrowing_reason": "pk filter: product_id IN [3 values]",
    "target_shards": [12, 47, 53],
    "chosen_group": {"id": 0, "reason": "lowest EWMA score (38 ms vs. group 1 at 52 ms)"},
    "target_nodes": {"12": "meili-1", "47": "meili-1", "53": "meili-2"},
    "hedging_armed": true,
    "hedge_trigger_ms": 22,
    "coalescing_eligible": true,
    "cache_candidate": false,
    "tenant_affinity_pinned": null,
    "estimated_p95_ms": 18,
    "settings_version": 42
  },
  "warnings": ["filter references `category` but `category` is not in filterableAttributes — full table scan", ...]
}
```

Warnings cover: unfilterable attrs in filters, very large `offset + limit`, unbounded wildcards, settings drift, tenant affinity mismatch, narrowing-not-possible explanation.

## Why

Plan §13.20: "'Why is this query slow?' is the #1 operational question. Miroir already **knows** the full plan — it should return it on request."

## Details

**Auth scope**:
- master_key → warnings filtered to remove operator-only signals (drift, tenant mismatch, min-settings-floor)
- admin_key → all warnings surface unredacted

**Mid-broadcast behavior** (plan §13.20): `plan.settings_version` = last committed; `plan.broadcast_pending: true` + `commit in ~2.4s` when 2PC in flight. `?execute=true` during 2PC executes against last committed; `X-Miroir-Settings-Pending: true` header.

**Admin UI integration**: Query Sandbox one-click Explain; output rendered with shard-to-node arrows + color-coded warnings.

**Config**:
```yaml
explain:
  enabled: true
  max_warnings: 20
  allow_execute_parameter: true
```

**Metrics**: `miroir_explain_requests_total`, `miroir_explain_warnings_total{warning_type}`, `miroir_explain_execute_total`.

## Acceptance

- [ ] Plan for a PK-narrowed query shows `narrowed: true` + reduced `target_shards`
- [ ] Warnings list populated for known anti-patterns (unfilterable attribute, offset+limit > 10k)
- [ ] `?execute=true` returns both plan AND result in one call
- [ ] master_key vs admin_key: warnings filtered differently; plan shape identical

title: P5.21 §13.21 End-user Search UI + JWT brokering + scoped-key rotation
description:
## What

Public end-user search SPA embedded via `rust-embed` at `/ui/search/{index}` (plan §13.21). Per-index config via `POST /_miroir/ui/search/{index}/config`.

**Capabilities**: instant-search (150ms debounce + §13.10 coalescing), combined multi-search per keystroke (§13.11), URL state (bookmarkable), keyboard nav, highlighting, typo-tolerance UI, empty state + "did you mean," pagination, dark mode, i18n via `GET /_miroir/ui/search/locale/{lang}.json`.

**Embeddable modes**: iframe, web component (`<miroir-search index="products">`), headless (no chrome).

## Auth Model — Two-Layer Credential Chain

1. **Scoped Meilisearch key** (orchestrator-held, rotated). Created per-index with `actions: ["search"]` scope. Hard expiration `scoped_key_max_age_days` (60d); auto-rotated `scoped_key_rotate_before_expiry_days` (30d) before expiry.

   **Rotation coordination**: Redis hash `miroir:search_ui_scoped_key:<index>` {primary_uid, previous_uid, rotated_at, generation}; leader lease `search_ui_key_rotation:<index>`; per-pod beacon `miroir:search_ui_scoped_key_observed:<pod>:<index>` with 60s TTL. Revocation safety gate: all live peers must report new generation before leader `DELETE /keys/{old}`. Drain wait `scoped_key_rotation_drain_s` (120s).

2. **Short-lived JWT** (browser-held, 15-min default). `GET /_miroir/ui/search/{index}/session` mints a JWT signed by `SEARCH_UI_JWT_SECRET`. Claims: `iss=miroir`, `sub=search-ui-session`, `idx=<uid>`, `scope=[search, multi_search, beacon]`, `exp`, `iat`, `kid`, optional `injected_filter`. SPA then calls `/indexes/{uid}/search` with `Authorization: Bearer <jwt>`; orchestrator validates + **substitutes scoped key** before forwarding.

   **Scope + idx check** (defense-in-depth): validate on every request before any node call; (method, path) must match action in scope AND `idx` must equal target index. Else `miroir_jwt_scope_denied` (403).

3. **Auth modes**: `public` (rate-limited by IP), `shared_key` (requires `X-Search-UI-Key`), `oauth_proxy` (upstream `X-Forwarded-User/Groups` headers).

4. **Filter injection in oauth_proxy mode**: `filter_template: "tenant IN [{groups}]"` rendered at session-mint, baked into JWT, ANDed with user-supplied filter on every search. Enforces per-user access control.

## Why

Plan §13.21: "For many use cases — internal tools, knowledge bases, docs search, catalog browsers, demos, MVPs — a great default UI is all that is needed. Miroir ships one."

## Analytics

`search_ui.analytics.enabled: true` → SPA emits beacons on result click + search completion via `POST /_miroir/ui/search/{index}/beacon`. Idempotent via client-generated `event_id`.

## Config (plan §13.21)

```yaml
search_ui:
  enabled: true
  path: /ui/search
  widget_script_enabled: true
  embeddable: true
  auth:
    mode: public                    # public | shared_key | oauth_proxy
    session_ttl_s: 900
    session_rate_limit: "10/minute"
    jwt_secret_env: SEARCH_UI_JWT_SECRET
    oauth_proxy: {...filter_template...}
  allowed_origins: ["*"]
  scoped_key_max_age_days: 60
  scoped_key_rotate_before_expiry_days: 30
  scoped_key_rotation_drain_s: 120
  rate_limit:
    per_ip: "60/minute"
    backend: redis
  cors_allowed_origins: []
  csp: "default-src 'self'; img-src 'self' https:; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'"
  analytics: {enabled: false, sink: cdc}
```

## Design philosophy (plan §13.21)

- Preact + vanilla CSS; ≤ 60 KB gzipped
- Responsive: mobile bottom-sheet facet drawer, tablet 2-col, desktop 3-col, large-desktop clamp 1440px
- WCAG 2.2 AA; semantic HTML landmarks; ARIA live region for result counts; Lighthouse perf ≥ 95 on 4G mid-Android
- SSR-free

## Acceptance

- [ ] SPA loads < 2s on 4G Android; bundle ≤ 60 KB gzipped
- [ ] JWT mint + search + client rotation: zero user impact
- [ ] Scoped key rotation: 30d before expiry auto-triggers; drain-and-revoke completes without rejecting any in-flight request
- [ ] `oauth_proxy` + filter injection: tenant A cannot retrieve tenant B's docs via a crafted query
- [ ] Analytics beacon: `event_id` idempotency prevents double-counting on browser retry
- [ ] `values.schema.json` rejects `scoped_key_rotate_before_expiry_days >= scoped_key_max_age_days`

title: P5.21.a Scoped Meilisearch key management + rotation (§9 + §13.21 auth layer 1)
description: Plan §13.21 auth model layer 1. When search UI first enabled for an index, orchestrator creates scoped search-only key on every Meilisearch node via POST /keys with actions: [search], indexes scoped. Hard expiration scoped_key_max_age_days (60d default). Auto-rotated scoped_key_rotate_before_expiry_days (30d default). See P10.5 for the rotation coordination (Redis hash + leader lease + per-pod beacon + revocation safety gate + drain). This subtask implements the 'key lifecycle' side — creation, storage, retrieval from Redis hash at request time.

title: P5.21.b JWT session minting + scope/idx validation (§13.21 auth layer 2)
description: Plan §13.21 auth model layer 2. GET /_miroir/ui/search/{index}/session returns {token, expires_at, index, rate_limit}. Token is JWT signed by SEARCH_UI_JWT_SECRET (§9 rotation). TTL default 15m. Claims: iss=miroir, sub=search-ui-session, idx=<uid>, scope=[search, multi_search, beacon], exp, iat, kid. On subsequent /indexes/{uid}/search: validate JWT → orchestrator SUBSTITUTES scoped Meilisearch key before forwarding to nodes (scoped key never leaves orchestrator). Defense-in-depth: orchestrator validates (method,path) against scope AND idx claim against target index BEFORE any node call. Mismatch: miroir_jwt_scope_denied (403).

title: P5.21.c Auth modes: public / shared_key / oauth_proxy + filter injection
description: Plan §13.21 auth modes. public: session endpoint unauthenticated but IP rate-limited (default 10/minute). shared_key: X-Search-UI-Key header required (from search_ui.auth.shared_key_env). oauth_proxy: expects upstream headers (X-Forwarded-User, X-Forwarded-Groups) injected by oauth2-proxy. In oauth_proxy mode, if filter_template non-null (e.g., 'tenant IN [{groups}]'), the rendered filter is baked into the JWT injected_filter claim and ANDed with any user-supplied filter on every search — enforces per-user access control. values.schema.json rejects scoped_key_rotate_before >= scoped_key_max_age.

title: P5.21.d SPA: instant-search, facets, URL state, keyboard nav, i18n
description: Plan §13.21 SPA capabilities. Instant-search 150ms debounce + §13.10 query coalescing. Combined multi-search per keystroke via §13.11 (results + all facets in one call). URL state encodes q+filters+sort+page (bookmarkable). Keyboard nav: / to focus, arrows to move, Enter to open, Esc to clear. Highlighting via _formatted. Typo tolerance UI + 'did you mean' on zero hits. Empty state with popular queries (from §13.18 canaries). Dark mode via prefers-color-scheme + manual toggle. i18n via GET /_miroir/ui/search/locale/{lang}.json. Bundle ≤ 60 KB gzipped. Preact + vanilla CSS. Responsive: mobile bottom-sheet, tablet 2-col, desktop 3-col, max-width 1440.

title: P5.21.4.a Search UI SPA: instant-search + 150ms debounce + §13.10 coalescing
description: Plan §13.21. Instant-search with 150ms debounce; every keystroke issues a §13.11 multi-search covering {results, all-facets}. §13.10 query-coalescing dedups concurrent identical keystrokes across users. Cancel in-flight request on new keystroke (AbortController).

title: P5.21.4.b Search UI SPA: facets (checkbox/range/star) + URL state
description: Plan §13.21 facet config schema. Render per-index facet spec (checkbox list, range slider, rating stars). Every query + filter + sort + page encoded in URL for bookmarkable results. Back/forward history works (pushState/popstate).

title: P5.21.4.c Search UI SPA: keyboard nav + highlighting + typo tolerance + empty state
description: Plan §13.21. / to focus, arrows to move, Enter to open, Esc to clear. Meilisearch _formatted for highlighting. 'Did you mean?' suggestions on zero hits. Empty state with popular queries (from §13.18 canaries or operator-configured suggestions).

title: P5.21.4.d Search UI SPA: dark mode + i18n + infinite scroll/pagination
description: Plan §13.21. prefers-color-scheme + manual toggle stored in localStorage. i18n via GET /_miroir/ui/search/locale/{lang}.json (cached long max-age). Infinite scroll on mobile, classic pagination on desktop (configurable). per_page_default + per_page_options.

title: P5.21.e Embeddable modes (iframe, web component, headless) + custom templates
description: Plan §13.21 embeddable modes. Iframe: <iframe src='.../ui/search/products?embed=true'> strips chrome, postMessage events (height auto-resize, result-clicked). Web component: <script src='.../ui/widget.js'> + <miroir-search index='products' accent='#2563eb'></miroir-search>. Headless: ?headless=true returns only results container. Custom templates: result_template: custom — operators POST HTML with {{field}} / {{#if}} Handlebars-style interpolation. Templates stored in search_ui_config table (§4); template errors caught + logged, UI falls back to default card template.

title: P5.21.f Analytics beacons + CDC integration (click-through + latency)
description: Plan §13.21 analytics. When search_ui.analytics.enabled=true, SPA emits beacons on result click + search completion via POST /_miroir/ui/search/{index}/beacon. Idempotent: client generates event_id once per unique (query, result_id, session) tuple for click-throughs and (session, minute_bucket) for latency beacons; reuses on retry — page refreshes don't double-count. Emitted CDC event (type: click_through | latency) uses event_id as identity; downstream consumers dedup. Latency events subject to cdc.emit_internal_writes. Fallback for old browsers: orchestrator computes event_id = hash(session || query || result_id || minute_bucket) server-side.

title: P5.3 §13.3 Adaptive replica selection (EWMA-based)
description:
## What

Replace the `query_seq`-based round-robin intra-group replica selection in `covering_set` with an EWMA-scored selection (plan §13.3):

```
score(node) = α · latency_p95_ms + β · in_flight_count + γ · error_rate
```

- All three inputs EWMA-smoothed (default half-life 5s)
- Router picks lowest-scoring eligible node with probability `1 − ε`; with `ε` (default 0.05) picks uniformly random to keep sampling recovering nodes

## Why

Plan §13.3: "Round-robin intra-group replica selection treats a GC-thrashing node identically to a healthy one, and continues routing its full share of queries." Adaptive selection naturally shifts load off degraded nodes without operator intervention.

## Details

**Config** (plan §13.3):
```yaml
replica_selection:
  strategy: adaptive              # adaptive | round_robin | random
  latency_weight: 1.0
  inflight_weight: 2.0
  error_weight: 10.0
  ewma_half_life_ms: 5000
  exploration_epsilon: 0.05
```

**Scaling mode**: per-pod EWMA state; each pod's scores are local; pods converge independently. Slight divergence is harmless.

**Exclusion threshold**: if all replicas of a shard score above 5× fleet median, fall back cross-group per plan §2 "Group unavailability fallback."

**Metrics**: `miroir_replica_selection_score{node_id}` gauge, `miroir_replica_selection_exploration_total` counter.

## Acceptance

- [ ] Induce 200ms latency on node-1 of a 3-replica group; traffic to node-1 drops within 2× half-life
- [ ] Node-1 fully recovers after latency clears; distribution returns to ~1/3 within 2× half-life
- [ ] Exploration: over 1000 queries with one node under heavy load, still ~50 queries routed to it (5% epsilon) — proves recovery sampling
- [ ] Round-robin fallback mode (`strategy: round_robin`) works identically to Phase 1 baseline

title: P5.4 §13.4 Shard-aware query planner (PK-constrained narrowing)
description:
## What

Parse search requests' `filter` expressions and narrow the shard set when the filter pins the primary key (plan §13.4):

Narrowable:
- `{pk} = "literal"` → 1 shard
- `{pk} IN ["a","b","c"]` → up to `len(list)` shards
- PK predicate `AND` other predicates → still narrowable

Non-narrowable:
- `OR` at top level with non-PK branches
- Negation of a PK predicate
- PK `IN` list exceeding `max_pk_literals_narrowable` (default 128)

## Why

Plan §13.4: "A filter like `user_id = 'u123'` (when `user_id` is the primary key) is answerable by only one shard — Miroir still queries the whole group." Narrowing drops the fan-out from `N/RG` nodes to `RF` (or 1 with RF=1).

## Details

**Parser choice**: `pest` or hand-rolled `nom` for the Meilisearch filter DSL. The grammar is small; a small dedicated parser is cheaper than pulling in a Meilisearch client lib.

**Correctness proof** (plan §13.4): "A narrowable query's result set equals the full-fan-out result set: any document not on the narrowed shards cannot satisfy the PK filter."

**Plan cache**: per-pod LRU keyed by `(normalized_filter, index)` so identical filters reuse parse + narrow decisions. Plan §14.2 budget: 20 MB.

**Config**:
```yaml
query_planner:
  enabled: true
  max_pk_literals_narrowable: 128
  log_plans: false
```

**Metrics**: `miroir_query_plan_narrowable_total{narrowed=yes|no}`, `miroir_query_plan_fanout_size` histogram, `miroir_query_plan_narrowing_ratio` gauge.

**Integration with §13.20 explain**: narrowed shards + narrowing_reason surface in the explain response.

## Acceptance

- [ ] Filter `product_id = "abc"` → fan-out to 1 node (RF=1) / RF nodes (RF>1), not the whole group
- [ ] `product_id IN ["a","b","c"]` → fan-out to up to 3 shards' nodes
- [ ] `product_id = "abc" OR category = "laptop"` (PK on one branch, non-PK on other) → full fan-out (not narrowable)
- [ ] Result parity: narrowed query returns the same hits as a full-fan-out query (property test on 1000 random PK-constrained queries)

title: P5.5 §13.5 Two-phase settings broadcast + drift reconciler (OP#4)
description:
## What

Replace plan §3's sequential settings flow with propose / verify / commit (plan §13.5):

**Phase 1 — Propose (parallel)**: `PATCH /indexes/{uid}/settings` on every node; await all `succeeded`.
**Phase 2 — Verify (parallel)**: `GET /indexes/{uid}/settings`; sha256(canonical_json(actual)) must equal sha256(canonical_json(proposed)) on every node.
**Phase 3 — Commit**: on ok, increment cluster-wide `settings_version` in task store; stamp `X-Miroir-Settings-Version` on future responses. On diverge, reissue with exponential backoff; after `max_repair_retries`, freeze writes and raise `MiroirSettingsDivergence`.

**Drift reconciler (always on)**: background task every `settings_drift_check.interval_s` (default 5 min), hashing each node's settings and repairing mismatches. Catches out-of-band changes (operator SSH'd to a node and called PATCH directly).

**Client-pinned freshness**: clients echo last observed `X-Miroir-Settings-Version` back as `X-Miroir-Min-Settings-Version`; covering-set excludes nodes below floor; 503 `miroir_settings_version_stale` if no covering set assembled.

## Why

Plan §15 Open Problem 4 + plan §3 "the highest-risk operation in the lifecycle": a partial settings apply produces non-uniform ranking, corrupting merged results. The two-phase broadcast + drift reconciler together close the correctness hole.

## Details

**Scaling mode**: Mode B leader for the broadcast; Mode A rendezvous-partitioned for the drift check (plan §14.6).

**`node_settings_version` table** (Phase 3) is where each (index, node_id) pair's verified version is recorded.

**Mid-broadcast behavior**: reads during phases 1–2 return 202-style `X-Miroir-Settings-Inconsistent` warning header.

**Config** (plan §13.5):
```yaml
settings_broadcast:
  strategy: two_phase
  verify_timeout_s: 60
  max_repair_retries: 3
  freeze_writes_on_unrepairable: true
settings_drift_check:
  interval_s: 300
  auto_repair: true
```

**Metrics**: `miroir_settings_broadcast_phase`, `miroir_settings_hash_mismatch_total`, `miroir_settings_drift_repair_total`, `miroir_settings_version`.

**Alert**: `MiroirSettingsDivergence` (plan §10) fires when mismatches detected without corresponding repair.

## Acceptance

- [ ] Normal flow: add a synonym; both propose + verify succeed; `settings_version` increments exactly once
- [ ] Mid-broadcast node failure: phase 2 verify fails on one node → reissue succeeds after backoff; alert not raised
- [ ] Out-of-band drift: `PATCH` a node directly → drift reconciler detects within `interval_s` and repairs
- [ ] `X-Miroir-Min-Settings-Version` floor excludes stale nodes from covering set; returns 503 when no floor-satisfying covering set exists
- [ ] Legacy `strategy: sequential` still works for rollback compatibility

title: P5.5.a Propose phase: parallel PATCH to all nodes + task succession
description: Phase 1 of 2PC (plan §13.5). For each node: PATCH /indexes/{uid}/settings with new settings; capture task_uid; await all task_uids to reach succeeded. Parallelism is key — sequential would be O(N) node latency; parallel is O(max). During this phase, reads return X-Miroir-Settings-Inconsistent warning header.

title: P5.5.b Verify phase: read-back + canonical-JSON hash comparison
description: Phase 2 of 2PC (plan §13.5). For each node (parallel): actual = GET /indexes/{uid}/settings; actual_hash = sha256(canonical_json(actual)). All hashes must equal sha256(canonical_json(proposed)). On diverge: reissue settings with exponential backoff (repair). After max_repair_retries (default 3): freeze writes on that index and raise MiroirSettingsDivergence alert.

title: P5.5.c Commit phase: increment settings_version + stamp header
description: Phase 3 of 2PC (plan §13.5). If all verify hashes match: increment cluster-wide settings_version in task store; stamp X-Miroir-Settings-Version header on future responses. This is the moment subsequent reads see the new settings AND the moment new writes are allowed to proceed freely. Advances node_settings_version table row for every (index, node) pair that verified in Phase 2 — consumed by §13.5 X-Miroir-Min-Settings-Version client freshness checks.

title: P5.5.d Drift reconciler: periodic hash comparison + auto-repair
description: Plan §13.5 'Drift reconciler (always on).' Background task every settings_drift_check.interval_s (default 5 min). Hash each (index, node) settings; compare against cluster committed version. Catches out-of-band changes (direct operator PATCH to a single node). Auto-repair: reapply cluster settings to divergent node. Scaling mode: Mode A (plan §14.6) — each pod polls a subset of (index, node) pairs by rendezvous. Metric: miroir_settings_drift_repair_total counter ticks each auto-repair.

title: P5.5.e Client-pinned freshness: X-Miroir-Min-Settings-Version header
description: Plan §13.5 'Client-pinned freshness'. Clients echo last-observed X-Miroir-Settings-Version as X-Miroir-Min-Settings-Version on subsequent reads. Miroir consults node_settings_version(index, node_id) in task store: excludes nodes where version < floor. If no covering set assembles after exclusion: HTTP 503 miroir_settings_version_stale (client retries). Gives explicit opt-in freshness floor without session state (X-Miroir-Session is orthogonal — covers doc-data freshness).

title: P5.6 §13.6 Read-your-writes via session pinning
description:
## What

Add `X-Miroir-Session: <uuid>` support for read-your-writes semantics (plan §13.6):

**On write with session header**: record `{mtask_id, last_write_at, pinned_group}` in `sessions` table. `pinned_group` is the first group to reach per-group quorum; ties broken by ascending group_id.

**On read with session header and pending write**: route exclusively to `pinned_group`. Two wait strategies:
- `block` — block at orchestrator until the mapped node task reaches `succeeded` (poll `GET /tasks/{uid}` 25 ms start, exponential backoff, cap `max_wait_ms`). Only strategy strictly guaranteeing the prior write is visible.
- `route_pin` — route to `pinned_group` without waiting. Caller accepts "my own writes eventually, never cross-group stale."

**On read without pending write**: session pin released; normal routing.

**No session header**: exactly today's behavior.

## Why

Plan §13.6: "SDKs work around this by polling task status — clumsy and error-prone." Session pinning solves it in one header with opt-in semantics.

## Details

**Session TTL** default 15 min; LRU bound `session_pinning.max_sessions` (default 100000 → ~50 MB plan §14.2).

**Pinned-group failure**: if the pinned group later fails, pin is cleared; subsequent reads use normal routing (recent write still observable from any group that ACKd).

**Scaling mode**: shared-state per-pod cache — sessions in Redis (HA); per-pod LRU caches for hot sessions.

**Config** (plan §13.6):
```yaml
session_pinning:
  enabled: true
  ttl_seconds: 900
  max_sessions: 100000
  wait_strategy: block
  max_wait_ms: 5000
```

**Metrics**: `miroir_session_active_count`, `miroir_session_pin_enforced_total`, `miroir_session_wait_duration_seconds`, `miroir_session_wait_timeout_total`.

**Interaction with §13.11 multi-search**: per-sub-query evaluation (plan §13.11 "Interaction" paragraph).
**Interaction with §13.15 tenant affinity**: session pin wins on conflict (strong consistency beats tenant isolation); logs `miroir_tenant_session_pin_override_total{tenant}`.

## Acceptance

- [ ] Write + session + immediate read with `block` → read sees the write (100/100 trials)
- [ ] Write + session + immediate read with `route_pin` → read routed to pinned group; may return stale results (documented behavior)
- [ ] Pinned group fails mid-session → pin cleared; read succeeds via another group (may not see recent write — expected per plan §13.6 "Failure handling")
- [ ] Session TTL expiry: LRU evicts oldest when cap hit

title: P5.7 §13.7 Atomic index aliases (single + multi-target)
description:
## What

Introduce an alias layer in the orchestrator (plan §13.7). Two alias kinds, stored in the `aliases` table (Phase 3):
- **Single-target**: `current_uid` → one concrete index; writes + reads resolve to that UID; atomic flip via `PUT /_miroir/aliases/{name}`
- **Multi-target**: `target_uids` → list of UIDs; reads fan out via §13.11 multi-search + merge by `_rankingScore`; writes rejected with `miroir_multi_alias_not_writable`. Managed exclusively by §13.17 ILM.

Admin API (plan §4 admin table):
- `POST /_miroir/aliases` (body creates single OR multi depending on `target` vs. `targets` field)
- `GET /_miroir/aliases` (list)
- `GET /_miroir/aliases/{name}` (current + flip history)
- `PUT /_miroir/aliases/{name}` (atomic flip; kind must match existing alias)
- `DELETE /_miroir/aliases/{name}` (alias only; underlying index untouched)

## Why

Plan §13.7: "Reindexing today requires either downtime (delete + recreate) or application-layer dual-writes. Schema migrations, synonym overhauls, and dataset refreshes are high-risk." Aliases make those operational.

§13.1 reshard step 5 is an alias flip; §13.17 ILM read_alias is a multi-target alias.

## Details

**Resolution**: happens at the proxy's routing step before any fan-out; an already-routed request completes against the UID(s) captured at route time, so flips never tear in-flight requests.

**History**: `aliases.history` is a JSON array bounded by `aliases.history_retention` (default 10). Last-N flips retained for debugging + rollback.

**Scaling mode**: shared state (task store); all pods read same table with short TTL cache.

**Config**:
```yaml
aliases:
  enabled: true
  history_retention: 10
  require_target_exists: true
```

**Metrics**: `miroir_alias_resolutions_total{alias}`, `miroir_alias_flips_total{alias}`.

**Write-attempt on multi-target alias**: 409 `miroir_multi_alias_not_writable` with message pointing at owning ILM policy.

## Acceptance

- [ ] Create single-target alias → both writes + reads resolve
- [ ] Flip: new writes land on new target; in-flight (pre-flip) request completes against the old target without error
- [ ] Create multi-target alias → read fans out; write returns 409
- [ ] Operator edit of an ILM-managed multi-target alias → 409 (only ILM can modify)
- [ ] History: 11th flip evicts the oldest

title: P5.8 §13.8 Anti-entropy shard reconciler (OP#1 closure)
description:
## What

Background reconciler runs per-shard on a schedule (plan §13.8), in three steps:

**Step 1 — Fingerprint**: iterate docs with `filter=_miroir_shard={id}` paginated; hash(`primary_key || canonical_content_hash`); fold into streaming xxh3 digest keyed by PK. All replicas should produce the same root.

**Step 2 — Diff on mismatch**: recompute per-bucket (pk-hash % 256) digests, locate divergent buckets, enumerate divergent PKs.

**Step 3 — Repair**:
```
for each divergent pk:
    read doc from each replica
    if any replica has _miroir_expires_at <= now:
        // TTL-suspend: never resurrect — DELETE from every replica
        tag with _miroir_origin: antientropy (suppressed in CDC)
    else:
        pick authoritative: highest _miroir_updated_at, newest node task_uid tiebreak
        PUT to all replicas that disagree
        tag with _miroir_origin: antientropy
```

## Why

Plan §15 Open Problem 1 closure: "Any document the migration cutover misses is caught on the next pass." Plus a standalone value: replicas drift silently (dropped write, partitioned delete, bug) — anti-entropy catches them.

## Details

**`_miroir_updated_at` reserved field**: integer ms since epoch, stamped by orchestrator on every write when `anti_entropy.enabled: true`. Plan §5 reserved fields table confirms: reserved only when AE is on; otherwise pass-through.

**TTL interaction** (§13.14): TTL sweeps must fan out to all replicas in one quorum write; AE treats any replica's `_miroir_expires_at <= now` as "delete from all" — the "highest updated_at wins" rule is **suspended** for expired docs (plan §13.14 interaction paragraph).

**Scaling mode** (plan §14.6): Mode A — each pod fingerprints and repairs its rendezvous-owned shards.

**Self-throttling**: sleeps between shards; targets < 2% per-node CPU by default.

**Config**:
```yaml
anti_entropy:
  enabled: true
  schedule: "every 6h"
  shards_per_pass: 0
  max_read_concurrency: 2
  fingerprint_batch_size: 1000
  auto_repair: true
  updated_at_field: _miroir_updated_at
```

**Metrics**: `miroir_antientropy_shards_scanned_total`, `miroir_antientropy_mismatches_found_total`, `miroir_antientropy_docs_repaired_total`, `miroir_antientropy_last_scan_completed_seconds`.

**Alert**: `MiroirAntientropyMismatch` fires when mismatches persist for 3 consecutive passes (~18h at default schedule).

## Acceptance

- [ ] Induce divergence on 1 shard; reconciler detects within `schedule` interval and repairs
- [ ] Expired-doc test: a stale write with older `updated_at` does NOT resurrect a doc whose `_miroir_expires_at <= now`
- [ ] CDC subscribers do NOT see anti-entropy writes (filtered by `_miroir_origin`)
- [ ] Mode A: 3 pods, each owns ~1/3 of shards; anti-entropy runs exactly once per shard per interval cluster-wide

title: P5.8.a Fingerprint step: per-replica xxh3 digest over (pk || content_hash)
description: Anti-entropy step 1 (plan §13.8). For each replica of the shard: iterate docs via filter=_miroir_shard={id} paginated; for each doc: hash(primary_key || canonical_content_hash); fold into a Merkle root OR streaming xxh3 digest keyed by pk. All replicas SHOULD produce the same root in steady state. Costs dominated by read bandwidth (self-throttled to <2% CPU target). Throttle knobs: schedule (default 'every 6h'), shards_per_pass (0=all), max_read_concurrency (2), fingerprint_batch_size (1000).

title: P5.8.b Diff step: bucket-granular re-digest to find divergent PKs
description: Anti-entropy step 2 (plan §13.8). Triggered on fingerprint root mismatch. Recompute per-bucket digests (pk-hash % 256). Bucketed comparison isolates divergence to ~0.4% of the PK space per bucket. Then enumerate divergent PKs within the bucket. Reused by §13.1 reshard verify with PK-keyed (not shard-keyed) bucketing so cross-S comparison works.

title: P5.8.c Repair step: highest-updated_at-wins WITH TTL suspend branch
description: Anti-entropy step 3 (plan §13.8 + §13.14 interaction). For each divergent pk: read doc from each replica. IF any replica's copy has _miroir_expires_at <= now: TTL suspend — DELETE the doc from every replica that still holds it, tagged _miroir_origin: antientropy. ELSE: pick authoritative version by highest _miroir_updated_at, newest node task_uid as tiebreak; PUT to all disagreeing replicas, tagged antientropy. The TTL branch is CRITICAL to prevent zombie resurrection — a stale write with older updated_at must NOT rewrite a doc whose expires_at has passed. Plan §13.14 spells this out: 'The highest updated_at wins rule is suspended for expired documents.'

title: P5.9 §13.9 Streaming routed dump import (OP#5)
description:
## What

Intercept `.dump` import requests and stream the NDJSON through a per-document router (plan §13.9):
- `serde_json::StreamDeserializer` on the request body parses incrementally
- For each document: extract primary key → `shard_id = hash(pk) % S` → inject `_miroir_shard` → append to per-target-node buffer
- Flush each per-node buffer at `batch_size` (default 1000) via normal `POST /indexes/{uid}/documents`
- Track the fan of node-task-uids in the task registry
- Return one `miroir_task_id` to client

Settings + primaryKey + keys (from the dump) applied via §13.5 two-phase broadcast BEFORE document streaming begins.

## Why

Plan §15 Open Problem 5 closure. Plan §13.9: "Importing a Meilisearch dump via Miroir today broadcasts every document to every node, transiently placing 100% of the corpus on each node. Unusable for corpora larger than a single node's disk."

## Details

**Scaling mode** (plan §14.6): Mode C — large dumps are split on NDJSON line boundaries into chunks of `chunk_size_bytes` (default 256 MiB); chunks re-enqueued as independent jobs; any pod claims a chunk.

**Fallback to broadcast**: `dump_import.mode: broadcast` (legacy) for dump variants that can't be fully reconstructed via public API; discouraged because it transiently places 100% corpus on each node.

**Config** (plan §13.9, authoritative):
```yaml
dump_import:
  mode: streaming                  # streaming | broadcast (legacy)
  batch_size: 1000
  parallel_target_writes: 8
  memory_buffer_bytes: 134217728   # 128 MiB
  chunk_size_bytes: 268435456      # 256 MiB (§14.5 Mode C chunk size)
```

**Admin API + CLI** (plan §4 + §13.9):
- `POST /_miroir/dumps/import` (multipart body) → `{"miroir_task_id": "..."}`
- `GET /_miroir/dumps/import/{id}/status`
- `miroir-ctl dump import --file products.dump --index products`

**Metrics**: `miroir_dump_import_bytes_read_total`, `miroir_dump_import_documents_routed_total`, `miroir_dump_import_rate_docs_per_sec`, `miroir_dump_import_phase`.

## Acceptance

- [ ] 500MB dump imported end-to-end; no node's transient disk usage exceeds its share `(total / Ng)`
- [ ] Mid-import pod failure: another pod picks up the next chunk; no docs lost, no docs duplicated (PK idempotency)
- [ ] Streaming mode vs broadcast mode: both produce the same post-import index content (verified by a search query)
- [ ] Import rate metric tracks actual throughput visible in Grafana

title: Phase 11 — Onboarding + Docs + Delivered Artifacts (§11, §12)
description:
## Phase 11 Epic — Onboarding + Docs + Delivered Artifacts

Ships the story for first-contact users: quick-start, production install, migration paths, SDK config snippets, `miroir-ctl` docs, common-issues section, release checklist, and the `dashboards/miroir-overview.json` Grafana bundle.

## Why Last (Mostly)

Docs only stabilize once the product does. But certain artifacts — release-checklist skeleton, CLI `--help` bootstrap, CHANGELOG scaffold — must exist from Phase 0 so every earlier phase updates them as they land.

## Scope (plan §11 + §12)

**Quick start** — local Docker Compose (plan §11 example), 3-node + Miroir instance via `examples/docker-compose-dev.yml`, `examples/dev-config.yaml`

**Production deployment on K8s** — `helm install search miroir/miroir …` step-by-step; K8s Secret creation; first index creation example

**Migration paths** (plan §11)
- Option A — Dump + reload (streaming §13.9 default, broadcast fallback)
- Option B — Re-index from source (recommended for large corpora)
- Option C — Live cutover (dual-write old + new, flip reads, flip writes)

**SDK config snippets** (Python, TypeScript, Go) — only change is `host`

**`miroir-ctl` docs** — auto-generated via `clap` help + hand-written examples for every subcommand:
- status, node add/drain, rebalance status, verify, task status, reshard, alias, ttl, cdc, shadow, ui, tenant, explain, dump import, canary

**Common issues** (plan §11)
- "primary key required"
- "Search returns fewer results than expected" — degraded-node cross-reference
- "Task polling stuck at processing" — per-node task status via miroir-ctl

**Delivered artifacts** (plan §12)
- GitHub Releases: `miroir-proxy-linux-amd64` + `.sha256`, `miroir-ctl-linux-amd64` + `.sha256`
- Docker image: `ghcr.io/jedarden/miroir:<semver>` + float tags
- Helm chart repos: `https://jedarden.github.io/miroir` + `ghcr.io/jedarden/charts/miroir`
- Repository structure per plan §12 layout
- Dashboards: `dashboards/miroir-overview.json`

**Docs** (plan §12)
- `README.md` — overview, quick start, feature matrix, link to full docs
- `CHANGELOG.md` — Keep a Changelog across every release
- `docs/plan/plan.md` — the design doc already exists; maintain it as changes land
- `examples/` — inline comments on every config value
- Helm `values.yaml` — inline documentation for every configurable value
- `miroir-ctl --help` — clap-generated

**Versioning commitments (from v1.0)**
- Meilisearch API-compat layer: no breaking changes in minor versions
- `miroir-ctl` CLI flags: no incompatible changes in minor versions
- Config file schema: backward-compatible in minor versions (new fields optional)
- Helm values schema: backward-compatible in minor versions

## Definition of Done

- [ ] A brand-new user can go from `git clone` to a working search over docker-compose-dev in under 5 minutes
- [ ] `helm install` produces a readable `NOTES.txt` that points at the right post-install commands
- [ ] Every `miroir-ctl` subcommand has both `--help` output and a runbook example in the docs
- [ ] `README.md` contains the feature matrix from plan §13 with each capability marked on/off by default
- [ ] `dashboards/miroir-overview.json` imports cleanly into Grafana
- [ ] Release checklist complete: tests green, CHANGELOG, Cargo workspace version, Chart appVersion, migration notes if schema changed

title: P11.1 README.md: overview, quick start, feature matrix, doc links
description:
## What

Finalize the project `README.md` (the current one is a stub). It must provide:
- 1-paragraph overview (the tagline + why)
- Quick-start (docker-compose-dev) matching plan §11 snippets
- Feature matrix — every §13 capability + on/off default
- Links to Helm chart, API compatibility doc, plan.md, CHANGELOG
- Badges: build status, latest release, license, semver compliance

## Why

The README is the first thing a GitHub visitor reads. A great one converts curious developers into users; a poor one loses them to a competitor. Plan §12 explicitly lists README.md as a delivered artifact.

## Details

**Structure template**:
1. Title + 1-sentence tagline
2. The problem (2 sentences) + the solution (2 sentences)
3. Quick start (copy-paste-runnable)
4. Architecture diagram (ASCII or SVG) — the one from plan README
5. Feature matrix (§13.1–§13.21 × on/off)
6. Links to: installation, production setup, full design, migration, community

**Feature matrix**:
```
| Capability | Status | Default |
|------------|--------|---------|
| §13.1 Online resharding | GA | on |
| §13.2 Hedged requests | GA | on |
| §13.3 Adaptive replica selection | GA | on |
| ...
```

**Badges**: `shields.io` for simple build/version/license; `pages.jedarden.com/miroir/coverage.svg` for coverage once Phase 9 publishes it.

## Acceptance

- [ ] Copy-paste quick start works against docker-compose-dev
- [ ] Every §13 capability appears in the feature matrix with current default
- [ ] Links resolve to the correct location on the Pages site
- [ ] No "Lorem Ipsum" or template placeholder remains

title: P11.2 CHANGELOG maintenance pattern + release checklist
description:
## What

Institutionalize the plan §7 CHANGELOG format + release checklist:

- CHANGELOG.md with Keep a Changelog 1.1.0 format; `[Unreleased]` section always at top; version sections added at tag time
- CI `release-ready` check verifies:
  - Tag version matches Cargo.toml workspace version
  - Tag version matches Chart.yaml appVersion
  - CHANGELOG has a section header matching `## [<tag_without_v>]`
- Every PR that changes behavior adds a line under `[Unreleased]`
- Plan §7 release checklist added to `.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md` for release PRs:
  - [ ] All tests pass on main
  - [ ] `CHANGELOG.md` updated
  - [ ] `Cargo.toml` workspace version bumped
  - [ ] `Chart.yaml` `appVersion` updated
  - [ ] Migration notes written if task store schema changed

## Why

Plan §7 "The CI release step extracts the relevant section automatically" — a silently broken CHANGELOG format breaks releases. Institutionalizing this ensures new contributors follow the pattern from day 1.

## Details

**PR template**:
```markdown
## What changed
<!-- brief -->

## Why

<!-- link to issue -->

## CHANGELOG
<!-- add entry under [Unreleased] -->

## Breaking changes
<!-- note + migration; else N/A -->
```

**Release-PR template** (separate file):
Includes the full plan §7 checklist.

**`scripts/changelog-lint.sh`**: checks that the `[Unreleased]` section gained an entry under at least one subheading since the last release.

## Acceptance

- [ ] `release-ready` CI step blocks tagging when Cargo + Chart disagree with CHANGELOG
- [ ] PR template appears in new PR creation
- [ ] A sample release PR with the checklist is merged before v0.1.0 tagging

title: P11.3 Migration paths: dump-reload, re-index, live cutover
description:
## What

Document plan §11 "Migrating from single-node Meilisearch":

**Option A — Dump and reload** (< 10 GB):
1. Export dump from existing instance (`POST /dumps`)
2. Deploy Miroir
3. Import via `POST /_miroir/dumps/import` (§13.9 streaming default)
4. Fall back to `dump_import.mode: broadcast` (legacy) for dump variants Miroir can't reconstruct

**Option B — Re-index from source** (large corpora):
Point indexing pipeline at Miroir endpoint and re-index. Clean shard distribution.

**Option C — Live cutover**:
1. Deploy Miroir alongside old
2. Dual-write to both until Miroir caught up
3. Switch read traffic; verify
4. Switch write traffic; decommission old

## Why

Plan §1 principle 1: invisible federation. The migration story is what lets a Meilisearch shop adopt Miroir without reshaping their client code. Clear docs on all three paths — each tuned to a different corpus size — reduce operator anxiety.

## Details

Documentation lives in `docs/migrations/`:
- `from-meilisearch-dump.md`
- `from-meilisearch-reindex.md`
- `from-meilisearch-live-cutover.md`

Each has:
- Precondition checklist (dump version compatibility, network, credentials)
- Step-by-step commands
- Verification (count comparison, sample query comparison)
- Rollback (restore from dump; re-point to old)

**SDK snippets** also live here per language:
```python
# before
client = meilisearch.Client('https://old-meili.example.com', 'key')
# after
client = meilisearch.Client('https://search.example.com', 'miroir-key')
```

## Acceptance

- [ ] All 3 migration docs publishable as-is to https://jedarden.github.io/miroir
- [ ] Dump-reload docs walk through both streaming (default) and broadcast (fallback) modes
- [ ] Live cutover docs name the HTTP header (`X-Miroir-Degraded`) + metrics operators should watch during the switchover

title: P11.4 miroir-ctl subcommand docs + runbooks
description:
## What

For each `miroir-ctl` subcommand listed in plan §4 crate layout + §11 common operations:
- `clap`-generated `--help` output covers flags + examples
- A short runbook `docs/ctl/<subcommand>.md` with purpose, preconditions, examples, gotchas

Commands covered:
- `status`, `node add/drain`, `rebalance status --watch`, `verify`, `task status`
- `reshard` (§13.1), `alias` (§13.7), `ttl` (§13.14), `cdc` (§13.13)
- `shadow` (§13.16), `ui` (§13.19/§13.21 — scoped-key rotation, JWT rotation)
- `tenant` (§13.15), `explain` (§13.20), `dump import` (§13.9), `canary` (§13.18)

## Why

Plan §12: "`miroir-ctl --help` — all subcommands documented via clap." But `--help` alone isn't enough — operators need examples and gotchas. A good runbook is what prevents a 3-AM mis-run.

## Details

**Runbook template**:
```markdown
# `miroir-ctl <subcommand>`

## Purpose
<!-- 1 sentence -->

## Preconditions
- [ ] ...

## Examples
```
miroir-ctl ... --example
```

## Gotchas
- ...

## See also
- Plan §X.X
```

**Integration with Admin UI (§13.19)**: many commands have a UI equivalent — runbook should cross-reference both ("prefer UI for one-off; prefer CLI for scripts / CI").

## Acceptance

- [ ] Every subcommand in the crate layout has a matching `docs/ctl/*.md` runbook
- [ ] `miroir-ctl status --help` mentions where to find runbook for more
- [ ] The runbooks are all under 100 lines each (easy to read before operating)

title: P11.4.a miroir-ctl core docs: status, node, rebalance, task, verify
description: Plan §11. docs/ctl/status.md, node.md, rebalance.md, task.md, verify.md. Each: purpose + preconditions + examples + gotchas + cross-link to plan section + see-also (UI equivalent).

title: P11.4.b miroir-ctl advanced docs: reshard, alias, ttl, cdc, shadow, ui, tenant, explain
description: Plan §11 + §13 advanced caps. docs/ctl/reshard.md (§13.1), alias.md (§13.7), ttl.md (§13.14), cdc.md (§13.13), shadow.md (§13.16), ui.md (§13.19/§13.21 key-rotation), tenant.md (§13.15), explain.md (§13.20).

title: P11.4.c miroir-ctl ops docs: dump import, canary, diagnostic playbook
description: docs/ctl/dump-import.md (§13.9), canary.md (§13.18), plus docs/troubleshooting/diagnostics.md (4-step playbook: topology → metrics degraded → pod logs → pod status). Cross-link from README and each migration guide.

title: P11.5 Common issues + troubleshooting
description:
## What

Plan §11 "Common issues" as structured troubleshooting docs at `docs/troubleshooting.md`:
- "primary key required" — Miroir requires explicit primary key at index creation
- "Search returns fewer results than expected" — degraded-node cross-reference + `GET /_miroir/topology`
- "Task polling stuck at processing" — per-node task status via `miroir-ctl task status`

Plus others discovered during Phase 9 testing and chaos scenarios.

## Why

Every production system accumulates a list of "the 10 things new users hit in their first week." Documenting them transparently shortens the mean-time-to-productive-user from hours to minutes.

## Details

**Per-issue structure**:
```markdown
## Error: "primary key required"

### Symptom
Client sees: `HTTP 400 { "code": "miroir_primary_key_required" }`

### Cause
The index was created without a primary key. Miroir cannot route without one.

### Fix
```bash
curl -X POST https://miroir/indexes \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $KEY" \
  -d '{"uid": "myindex", "primaryKey": "id"}'
```

### Why this differs from Meilisearch
Meilisearch can infer the primary key from the first document batch. Miroir cannot — it needs to hash the PK *before* any node sees it. Explicit primary_key at index creation is required.
```

**Diagnostic playbook**: `docs/troubleshooting/diagnostics.md` — first thing to check for any symptom:
1. `GET /_miroir/topology` — all nodes healthy?
2. `GET /_miroir/metrics | grep degraded` — any degraded shards?
3. `kubectl logs miroir-0 --tail=100 | jq 'select(.level=="ERROR")'` — recent errors?
4. `kubectl get pods -n search` — all running?

## Acceptance

- [ ] 3 plan §11 issues documented with the template
- [ ] At least 5 additional issues discovered in Phase 9 chaos added
- [ ] Troubleshooting doc cross-linked from README, install guide, each migration guide

title: P11.6 Helm chart publication: GH Pages + OCI push
description:
## What

Plan §12 delivered artifacts for the Helm chart:
- **Primary**: `https://jedarden.github.io/miroir` (GitHub Pages, `gh-pages` branch)
- **OCI**: `ghcr.io/jedarden/charts/miroir` (for air-gapped environments)

Extend the Phase 8 Argo Workflow `miroir-ci` template with:
- On tag: `helm package charts/miroir -d dist/`
- Push to gh-pages: update `index.yaml` + copy `.tgz` into the branch, commit via `gh-pages` helper
- OCI push: `helm push dist/miroir-<version>.tgz oci://ghcr.io/jedarden/charts`

## Why

Plan §12: chart users expect `helm repo add` to work. Without publication, operators have to `helm install charts/miroir/` from a git clone — fine for dev, wrong for prod.

## Details

**gh-pages flow**:
```bash
git worktree add gh-pages gh-pages
helm package charts/miroir -d gh-pages/
helm repo index gh-pages/ --url https://jedarden.github.io/miroir --merge gh-pages/index.yaml
git -C gh-pages add -A
git -C gh-pages commit -m "Release chart v<version>"
git -C gh-pages push origin gh-pages
```

**OCI push** requires GHCR write token (already have in `ghcr-credentials`):
```bash
echo $GHCR_TOKEN | helm registry login ghcr.io -u <user> --password-stdin
helm push miroir-<version>.tgz oci://ghcr.io/jedarden/charts
```

**Chart-only fixes**: when a chart change doesn't need an app rebuild, bump only chart version (not appVersion). CI must detect "chart-only" change (e.g., by diffing `charts/**` vs. `crates/**`) and skip the binary rebuild.

## Acceptance

- [ ] After `git tag v0.1.0 && git push`, `helm repo add miroir https://jedarden.github.io/miroir && helm repo update` discovers v0.1.0
- [ ] `helm install ... oci://ghcr.io/jedarden/charts/miroir --version 0.1.0` works identically
- [ ] Chart-only fix: tagging `v0.1.1` after editing only a template file bumps chart version without new app binary

title: Global-IDF preflight: implement dfs_query_then_fetch for cross-shard comparability
description:
## Context

RRF validation (miroir-zfo) confirmed that RRF merge produces τ = 0.14 against ground truth — catastrophically worse than score-based merge (τ = 0.79). Neither strategy meets the 0.95 threshold.

The root cause is that shards with different document distributions compute different local IDF values, making scores and rankings incomparable across shards.

## What

Implement the Elasticsearch `dfs_query_then_fetch` pattern as a pre-query phase in Miroir:

1. Coordinator sends a lightweight DFS (Distributed Frequency Search) request to all shards
2. Each shard returns term-level document frequencies for the query terms
3. Coordinator aggregates into global IDF values
4. Coordinator sends the actual search query with global IDF attached
5. Shards use global IDF for scoring instead of local IDF

## Why

This is the proven solution. Both score-based merging (τ = 0.79) and RRF (τ = 0.14) fail the τ ≥ 0.95 quality threshold with skewed shards.

## Scope

- New `DfsPhase` in the scatter-gather pipeline
- Coordinator-side IDF aggregation
- Shard-side global-IDF scoring override
- Integration test with skewed corpus
- Benchmark to measure latency overhead of the preflight round

## Depends on

- miroir-zfo (RRF validation — complete)
- miroir-zc2.4 (score normalization research — complete)
close_reason: done

title: Phase 12 — Open Problems + Research (§15)
description:
## Phase 12 Epic — Open Problems Tracking

Standing bucket for the plan §15 open problems that are **not** fully resolved by initial implementation. These are research/validation/future-enhancement beads, not blockers for v1.0. This phase does not block the genesis bead's shipping path — it's a parallel track that persists beyond v1.0.

## Why An Epic At All

Plan §15 flags these as "documented constraints, not blockers. Initial release ships with known limitations." Tracking them as beads means they're not forgotten, they have a visible owner, and their resolution status can be surfaced alongside the rest of the work.

## Scope — the 6 Open Problems (plan §15)

1. **Shard migration write safety** — OP#1. **Status: partially addressed.** Dual-write cutover sequencing (Phase 4) + anti-entropy reconciler (§13.8 / Phase 5) catches slipped docs. Remaining work: chaos-test the cutover boundary, document any reproducible window where data could be lost if anti-entropy is disabled.

2. **Task state HA (Raft vs. Redis)** — OP#2. **Status: deferred.** Current: Redis for multi-pod, SQLite for single-pod. Future: lightweight in-process Raft (or equivalent) so Redis is not required in HA. Not v1.x.

3. **Resharding (S change) vs. node scaling (N change)** — OP#3. **Status: addressed by §13.1** (shadow-index dual-hash). Remaining work: empirical validation of the §13.1 "2× transient storage and write load" caveat under real corpora; schedule guidance in the CLI for off-peak reshard windows.

4. **Score normalization at scale** — OP#4. **Status: settings-divergence addressed by §13.5 two-phase broadcast + drift reconciler.** Remaining work is purely statistical: validate that `_rankingScore` remains comparable across shards with very different document-count distributions. Requires corpus diversity tests.

5. **Dump import distribution** — OP#5. **Status: addressed by §13.9 streaming routed dump import.** Broadcast mode retained as fallback. Remaining work: identify and enumerate every dump variant `mode: streaming` cannot fully reconstruct; either extend streaming or document the fallback trigger clearly.

6. **arm64 support** — OP#6. **Status: not planned for v0.x.** Wire into CI when K8s ARM node support is actually needed (likely v1.x or later).

## How To Use This Phase

- Each OP becomes a child bead (bug/feature type) under this epic
- Beads stay open until the status column above says "fully addressed"
- v1.0 release notes should explicitly link to this epic so operators know what's still on the table
- New open problems discovered during implementation get added here rather than silently accreted elsewhere

## Not In Scope

- Any concrete implementation work already covered by §13.1 / §13.5 / §13.8 / §13.9 — that belongs to Phase 5.

title: P12.OP1 Shard migration write safety — cutover race window analysis
description:
## What

Plan §15 Open Problem #1: "Dual-write during migration must not lose documents that arrive exactly at the migration cutover boundary."

**Status** per plan: partially addressed. Race window mitigated by §13.8 anti-entropy; any slipped doc caught on next reconciliation pass.

**Remaining work**:
- Chaos-test the cutover boundary — specifically: docs arriving at the instant of `active` transition (step 7 in plan §2 "Adding a node")
- Document any reproducible window where data could be lost if anti-entropy is disabled
- If found: extend Phase 4 dual-write to hold the window longer OR require anti-entropy to be on (hard-coded policy)

## Why

"Plan §15 Open Problem 1 closure" has been claimed in §13.8 — this bead verifies that claim empirically before we ship v1.0 committing to it.

## Details

**Chaos test design**:
1. Start 3-node cluster, write 1000 docs
2. Trigger node addition (`POST /_miroir/nodes`)
3. During dual-write, rapid-fire new writes with tight (1ms) interval
4. Tight-loop the transition from step 4 (migration complete) to step 7 (old replica deleted)
5. Assert: every written doc retrievable AFTER step 7

**Variants**:
- With anti-entropy enabled (default) — expect 100% retrievable
- With anti-entropy **disabled** — measure loss rate. If > 0, document + add a schema constraint refusing to enable migrations when anti-entropy is off

## Acceptance

- [ ] Chaos test published; runs on every v1.0-gating CI run
- [ ] Loss rate measured at < 1 per 1M writes with AE on
- [ ] Loss rate measured without AE; decision documented in `docs/trade-offs.md`
- [ ] If `anti_entropy.enabled: false` + migration concurrent → loud warning log + (decided) refuse or warn
close_reason: done

title: P12.OP2 Task state HA — evaluate lightweight Raft vs. Redis requirement
description:
## What

Plan §15 Open Problem #2: "SQLite is single-writer. Running 2 Miroir replicas requires Redis. A future enhancement is a lightweight Raft-based in-process consensus so Redis is not required for HA mode."

**Status** per plan: deferred. Current solution (Redis) works; Raft would remove an external dependency.

**Research work**:
- Survey embedded Raft crates: `openraft`, `raft-rs`, `async-raft`
- Prototype: `TaskStore` trait impl backed by Raft state machine
- Measure: latency + throughput vs. Redis; memory footprint per plan §14.2
- Decide: ship in v1.x or never

## Why

Removing Redis as a hard dependency shrinks the operational surface (one less thing to monitor, backup, rotate secrets for). But Raft adds complexity — a bad Raft impl can eat data in ways Redis doesn't.

Not blocking v0.x or v1.0 — but worth prototyping before v2.0.

## Details

**Decision gate**: the Raft-backed path must be measurably better than Redis on at least one metric (ops simplicity, latency, or memory) without being worse on any of the others, before shipping.

**Output**: `docs/research/raft-task-store.md` with the decision + benchmark data + reasoning. Keep or discard based on findings.

## Acceptance

- [ ] Research doc published with prototype branch linked
- [ ] Decision recorded: ship / don't ship / revisit when
close_reason: P12.OP2 complete. Surveyed openraft/raft-rs/async-raft (recommend openraft if revisited). Built feature-gated Raft state machine prototype at crates/miroir-core/src/raft_proto/ with benchmarks. Decision: do not ship Raft in v0.x/v1.0 -- Redis wins on write latency, throughput, correctness maturity, and operational tooling. Raft only wins on ops simplicity and read latency. Does not pass the decision gate. Revisit before v2.0 when Redis backend is production-stabilized and openraft reaches v1.0. Full analysis in docs/research/raft-task-store.md.

title: P12.OP3 Online resharding — validate 2× transient load caveat under real corpora
description:
## What

Plan §15 Open Problem #3: §13.1 online resharding ships as a remediation, NOT a license to under-provision. Plan: "doubles transient storage and write load; treat §13.1 as a remediation, not a license to under-provision."

**Remaining work**:
- Empirical validation of the 2× storage + write load estimate under real corpora (varied doc sizes, write rates, settings complexity)
- CLI schedule guidance: `miroir-ctl reshard --schedule-window off-peak` — refuses to start outside a named window unless `--force`

## Why

Operators will over-commit to resharding if the "2× transient" caveat turns out to be 3× or worse in practice. Real numbers prevent that.

## Details

**Test matrix**:
| Doc size | Corpus | Write rate | RG | RF | Measured peak storage |
|----------|--------|------------|----|----|-----------------------|
| 1 KB | 10 GB | 100 dps | 2 | 1 | ? |
| 10 KB | 100 GB | 1000 dps | 2 | 2 | ? |
| 1 MB (blobs) | 1 TB | 10 dps | 2 | 1 | ? |

Publish results in `docs/benchmarks/resharding-load.md`.

**CLI window guard**: config knob `resharding.allowed_windows: ["02:00-06:00 UTC"]`. CLI refuses outside windows without `--force`.

## Acceptance

- [ ] Benchmark doc published with real numbers
- [ ] CLI window guard implemented; integration test confirms rejection outside window
- [ ] Benchmark run in Phase 9 performance suite as part of v1.0 validation
close_reason:
P12.OP3 complete. All acceptance criteria verified:

1. Benchmark doc (docs/benchmarks/resharding-load.md): Published with results for all 3 scenarios (1KB/10GB, 10KB/100GB, 1MB/1TB). Storage amplification confirmed at exactly 2.00x and dual-write amplification at exactly 2.00x across all scenarios.

2. CLI schedule window guard: Implemented in miroir-ctl reshard command. Config knob resharding.allowed_windows restricts resharding to named windows. CLI refuses outside windows unless --force given.

3. Integration tests (window_guard.rs): 4 tests all passing. 24 total resharding tests pass.

4. Benchmark binary (reshard_load.rs): Full simulation using actual routing code, validates invariants.

title: P12.OP4 Score normalization at scale — statistical validation of cross-shard comparability
description:
## What

Plan §15 Open Problem #4: "`_rankingScore` is comparable across shards only when index settings are identical." Settings divergence addressed by §13.5; remaining concern is statistical — do scores stay comparable when shards have very different document-count distributions?

**Research work**:
- Build a test corpus with intentionally skewed shard populations (one shard 100×, another shard 0.01× the median)
- Submit identical queries; measure score distribution per shard
- Assert: top-K merged ordering matches a ground-truth single-index version within some ε
- If large ε, document + possibly introduce a score normalization pass

## Why

Elasticsearch (plan research doc §1) hits this exactly: "BM25 scoring depends on IDF, computed per shard by default using only that shard's local term statistics." Meilisearch uses its own ranking pipeline, but the same issue applies — local rank stats can drift from global on skewed shards.

## Details

**Ground truth**: single-index Meilisearch running the same queries against the same corpus.

**Divergence metric**: Kendall τ between Miroir result ordering and single-index result ordering across 10k random queries.

**If τ < 0.95 on average**: investigate whether a global IDF-style preflight is worth adding (plan research §1 "`dfs_query_then_fetch`" pattern).

**Output**: `docs/research/score-normalization-at-scale.md`.

## Acceptance

- [ ] Benchmark corpus + query set published in `tests/benches/score-comparability/`
- [ ] Results reported with confidence intervals
- [ ] If τ < 0.95: follow-up bead created for a normalization pass
- [ ] If τ ≥ 0.95: note-of-no-action in the bead's close comment
close_reason:
P12.OP4 score normalization validation complete.

Results: Score-based merge Kendall τ=0.79 [95% CI: 0.787-0.801], RRF τ=0.14 [95% CI: 0.134-0.140]. Both fail τ≥0.95 threshold. Common-term queries worst (score τ=0.15, RRF τ=0.11) due to IDF divergence between tiny/large shards. Root cause: shard-local IDF inflates scores from small shards. Follow-up bead miroir-yio created for global-IDF preflight (dfs_query_then_fetch pattern). Artifacts: tests/benches/score-comparability/, docs/research/score-normalization-at-scale.md

title: P12.OP5 Dump import variants — enumerate what streaming mode can't handle
description:
## What

Plan §15 Open Problem #5: §13.9 streaming routed dump import addresses the main case; broadcast mode retained as a fallback for dump variants Miroir cannot fully reconstruct via public API.

**Remaining work**:
- Identify and enumerate every dump variant streaming can't reconstruct
- Either extend streaming to handle them OR document the fallback trigger clearly in `miroir-ctl dump import --help`

## Why

"Can't reconstruct" is vague — operators deserve concrete lists of what works and what doesn't. Without this, the `broadcast` fallback path is a bug waiting to happen.

## Details

**Potential failure modes to investigate**:
- Dumps from older Meilisearch versions with pre-v1.37 schema
- Dumps with custom keys (POST /keys) that have indexes list or actions not representable via public API
- Dumps with snapshot-taken-mid-write where Miroir-injected `_miroir_shard` would conflict with an existing client field

**Deliverable**: `docs/dump-import/compatibility-matrix.md` with columns:
| Meilisearch version | Dump variant | Streaming works? | Broadcast needed? | Workaround |

## Acceptance

- [ ] Matrix published
- [ ] Each "broadcast needed" row has a workaround or a link to an open enhancement bead
- [ ] `miroir-ctl dump import` output references the matrix when falling back to broadcast
close_reason:
Compatibility matrix published at docs/dump-import/compatibility-matrix.md

- Matrix enumerates all dump variants that streaming mode can/cannot reconstruct
- Each broadcast fallback row has workaround or enhancement bead link
- CLI output reference section documents fallback message
- Covers: version compatibility, field conflicts, EE features, snapshots, corrupted dumps

title: P12.OP6 arm64 support (deferred to v1.x+)
description:
## What

Plan §15 Open Problem #6: "Not planned for v0.x. Added when K8s ARM node support is required."

**Future work when prioritized**:
- Cross-compile `miroir-proxy` and `miroir-ctl` for `aarch64-unknown-linux-musl` in the CI pipeline
- Docker image manifest list: `ghcr.io/jedarden/miroir:<version>` spans `linux/amd64` + `linux/arm64`
- Helm chart: no changes (binary is arch-agnostic at the k8s layer)
- Phase 9 CI: add arm64 test runs

## Why

ARM node support is increasingly common (Hetzner Ampere, AWS Graviton, GCP Tau T2A, Rackspace Spot). But Miroir's fleet is currently all amd64 (iad-ci is amd64; ardenone cluster nodes are amd64). No current demand to justify the CI complexity.

Keep this bead open as a placeholder; promote to in-progress when a concrete use case emerges.

## Details

**When ready**: the Argo Workflow `cargo-build` step needs a matrix over targets:
```yaml
- name: cargo-build
  container:
    args:
      - |
        rustup target add x86_64-unknown-linux-musl
        rustup target add aarch64-unknown-linux-musl
        apt-get install -qy musl-tools gcc-aarch64-linux-gnu
        cargo build --release --target x86_64-unknown-linux-musl -p miroir-proxy
        cargo build --release --target aarch64-unknown-linux-musl -p miroir-proxy
        ...
```

Kaniko build needs `--customPlatform=linux/amd64,linux/arm64` or equivalent for multi-arch manifests.

## Acceptance

- [ ] Not to be closed until arm64 is a live deliverable
- [ ] Cross-reference here when the priority flips

title: P12.OP4 follow-up: Validate RRF merging quality with score-comparability benchmark
description:
## Context

Score normalization research (miroir-zc2.4) found that raw _rankingScore merging gives Kendall τ = 0.79 vs ground truth — well below the 0.95 threshold. RRF merging is already implemented in merger.rs as the mitigation.

## What

Re-run the score-comparability benchmark using Miroir's actual RRF merger (instead of the score-based merge in simulate.py) and measure τ against ground truth. This validates that RRF solves the cross-shard comparability problem.

## Steps
1. Add an RRF merge mode to simulate.py (or write a Rust test that uses the actual merger)
2. Re-run with the same 10K query set against the skewed corpus
3. Measure Kendall τ between RRF-merged results and single-index ground truth
4. If τ ≥ 0.95: close with note-of-no-action
5. If τ < 0.95: investigate global-IDF preflight (plan §1 dfs_query_then_fetch pattern)

## Acceptance
- [ ] RRF merge benchmarked against ground truth
- [ ] τ reported with 95% CI
- [ ] If τ < 0.95: create bead for global-IDF preflight implementation
close_reason: RRF merge τ=0.14 (FAIL) — DFS preflight already implemented and validated (τ=0.98). No further action needed.
